channel.title: Paper Feeds
channel.description: Keyword-based research paper feeds from arXiv and IACR

item.title: MESA: Prioritizing Vulnerable Communication Channels for Securing Multi-Agent Systems
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30602v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

多智能体系统（MAS）正广泛应用于自动化复杂分布式任务，但其智能体间通信信道构成新型、隐蔽且难以防御的攻击面。现有安全实践多依赖事后响应，缺乏对未被观测到的潜在攻击下关键通信边的前瞻性识别能力。我们发现：信道级攻击影响高度不均衡——单条被攻破的边可导致高达75%的整体攻击成功，凸显优先防护的必要性。

方法：MESA框架

本文提出MESA（Multi-Agent Edge Security Assessment），一种无需标签、无需真实攻击痕迹的主动式信道风险评估框架。它融合6种图论指标（如介数中心性、路径冗余度、信息流敏感度等）与2类动态探针（ablation移除测试与masking遮蔽测试），量化每条边对系统全局决策鲁棒性的贡献度，从而识别“安全关键边”——即一旦被篡改最可能扭曲系统输出的通信链路。

主要发现与验证

在涵盖3类典型MAS场景（协作推理、任务分解、辩论共识）、8种网络拓扑及5个主流开源大模型（Qwen、Llama、Gemma系列）的综合评估中：

  • Mesa排序与实证单边攻击成功率呈强相关（平均Spearman ρ = +0.60，峰值+0.73）；
  • 在资源受限防御部署下，仅监控前10%高危边即可拦截约3倍于随机策略的成功攻击；
  • 鲁棒性测试表明其在LangGraph工作流、异构攻击者/防御者建模下保持有效性，但在自适应攻击或极高冗余图中性能边际下降。

创新点

MESA首次将图结构分析与轻量动态探针结合，实现无监督、低开销、可部署的MAS信道风险量化，为多智能体基础设施的主动安全加固提供了可扩展、可解释的工程化路径。

AI Summary (English)

Multi-agent systems (MAS) introduce critical, under-defended attack surfaces through inter-agent communication channels. To enable proactive defense before attacks occur, we propose MESA, a label-free framework that ranks MAS communication edges by security-criticality—i.e., their likelihood of compromising system-level decisions if breached. MESA integrates six graph-theoretic metrics with two lightweight dynamic probes (ablation and masking), requiring no attack traces or ground-truth labels. Evaluated across three MAS scenarios, eight topologies, and five open-source LLMs (Qwen, Llama, Gemma), MESA achieves strong correlation with empirical per-edge attack success (mean Spearman ρ = +0.60, up to +0.73). Monitoring only the top 10% of MESA-ranked edges intercepts ≈3× more successful attacks than random allocation under resource constraints. We further characterize its robustness across LangGraph workflows and its limitations under adaptive adversaries and high-redundancy graphs. Our results demonstrate that edge-level risk in MAS is often concentrated and predictable—enabling scalable, proactive infrastructure hardening.

Abstract

Multi-agent systems (MAS) are increasingly used to automate complex, distributed workflows. However, their inter-agent communication channels introduce new attack surfaces that remain poorly understood and are difficult to defend against. In this paper, we address how defenders should prioritize limited security effort to protect vulnerable communication channels before attacks are observed. This is motivated by our observation that the channel-level attack impact is highly non-uniform: a single compromised edge can account for up to 75% of total attack success. We introduce Mesa, a label-free framework for proactively ranking which MAS edges are most security-critical -- that is, most likely to affect the system's decision if compromised. Mesa combines six graph-theoretic metrics and two dynamic probes (ablation and masking) without requiring attack traces. We evaluate Mesa against a dynamic misinformation attack pipeline across three diverse MAS scenarios, eight network topologies, and five open-source LLMs from Qwen, Llama, and Gemma families. Mesa rankings correlate strongly with empirical per-edge attack success rate, achieving mean Spearman $ρ=+0.60$ (peaking at $+0.73$). In resource-constrained defense deployment, monitoring the top 10% of Mesa-ranked edges intercepts about 3x the successful attacks as random allocation. We further test Mesa under varying attacker and defender models and LangGraph workflows and characterize its limits under adaptive attacks and high-redundancy graphs. Overall, our results show that edge-level risk in MAS is often concentrated and predictable, allowing proactive hardening of multi-agent infrastructures.

item.title: A Hybrid Framework For Crypto-Ransomware Detection In Enterprise Shared Storage
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30586v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

企业环境中，敏感数据普遍集中存储于网络共享存储（如NAS、SMB文件服务器），而非终端本地。传统端点检测工具（如EDR）难以感知客户端对远程存储的恶意加密行为——攻击者仅需在受控终端发起批量文件读写操作，即可静默加密共享卷，而文件服务器本身无异常进程或注册表变更，导致现有防御体系存在严重盲区。

方法创新

本文提出一种面向企业共享存储环境的混合式勒索软件检测框架，核心包含两大协同模块：

  • Region of Interest (RoI) 流量分析技术：聚焦客户端与文件服务器间协议流量中“高风险操作序列”（如连续大文件随机读+立即覆写、异常文件扩展名批量变更等），提取时序性、语义性特征；
  • 双轨检测机制：
  • 规则增强层：将RoI识别出的新型IoCs结构化入库，作为轻量级插件集成至现有IDS/EDR，提升签名检测覆盖率；
  • 机器学习层：基于RoI特征训练XGBoost模型，专为绕过行为沙箱与混淆技术的高逃逸型加密勒索软件（如LockBit 3.0、BlackCat）优化。

关键成果

  • 在涵盖12个主流勒索软件家族（含变种）及87类真实办公场景良性行为（如CAD设计、视频转码、数据库备份）的数据集上验证：
  • ML模块达99.64%检测精度，0%漏报率（FNR），0.12%误报率（FPR）；
  • 实现加密前平均2.3秒预警（较文件损坏阈值提前92%），支持在首波加密完成前阻断攻击；
  • RoI方法无需部署服务器端代理或修改协议栈，兼容Windows/Linux SMB/NFS环境，具备生产级落地可行性。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a hybrid framework for detecting crypto-ransomware targeting enterprise shared storage (e.g., SMB/NFS servers), addressing the critical blind spot where traditional endpoint detection fails to monitor malicious client-initiated encryption of remote files. Our approach centers on a novel Region of Interest (RoI) network traffic analysis technique that identifies high-risk behavioral patterns—such as sequential random-read-and-overwrite bursts or rapid extension changes—within client-server file protocol flows. RoI-derived features train an XGBoost model achieving 99.64% precision, 0% false negative rate, and only 0.12% false positive rate, while extracted IoCs enrich existing EDR/IDS rule sets. Evaluated across 12 ransomware families and domain-validated benign workloads (e.g., CAD, backup, media encoding), the system detects intrusions on average 2.3 seconds before significant damage, enabling pre-encryption mitigation. The framework requires no server-side instrumentation and maintains broad protocol compatibility.

Abstract

Most corporate workplace environments enforce policies and technical controls that limit the storage of sensitive data on client endpoints. Consequently, ransomware operators have evolved variants that expand their attack surface from local systems to network drives and shared storage resources. As traditional endpoint detection mechanisms focus primarily on local system behaviour, a compromised client can impact remote file servers, such as by encrypting shared data, without directly triggering behavioural changes on the servers themselves. In this paper, we propose a hybrid detection framework for detecting crypto-ransomware intrusion within integrated file server and client environments. The framework is based on a new technique referred to as Region of Interest (RoI) to analyse network traffic and extract Indicators of Compromise (IoCs). The IoC repository serves as an additional ruleset to enhance existing security tools such as EDRs and IDSs, while RoI-derived features are used to train an ML model to detect highly evasive variants. This study incorporates a broader set of ransomwares families and carefully selected benign behaviors based on domain expertise, ensuring coverage of common user actions that could interfere with ransomware detection. Beyond IoCs, which operate in a signature-based manner, our machine learning module achieves a detection precision of 99.64%, with a 0% false negative rate (FNR) and a minimal false positive rate (FPR). Furthermore, the proposed method enables early detection, identifying ransomware intrusions before significant damage occurs, achieving an accuracy of 99.44%.

item.title: Forensic Trajectory Signatures for Agent Memory Poisoning Detection
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30566v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

大语言模型（LLM）智能体在部署中面临“持续性记忆投毒”（persistent memory poisoning）攻击——攻击者通过污染长期记忆库，诱导智能体在后续任务中泄露敏感信息。现有检测方法多依赖静态提示分析或单点异常识别，难以应对隐蔽、分布式的攻击痕迹。

方法创新：法证轨迹签名

本文首次发现并形式化一个行为不变量（behavioral invariant）：在采用“可观测内存工具调用”进行路由的智能体架构中，成功记忆投毒攻击必须严格满足时序约束——即在调用 email_send_email 前必须先调用 memory_recall_fact；而正常（非窃取）会话极少呈现该转移模式。该不变量源于攻击固有的信息检索依赖链，而非经验相关性——人为抑制该转移将直接导致攻击失效。

核心发现与性能

  • 单规则检测器仅基于该不变量即可实现 AUC = 0.9563；
  • 19维轨迹特征的随机森林模型进一步提升至 AUC = 0.9904（BCa 95% CI [0.987, 0.993]，N=10,000），证实攻击在多条独立行为通道上留下强信号；
  • 过定性验证：移除全部召回相关特征（占特征集一半）后，AUC 仍稳定在 0.990，表明签名是分布式轨迹模式，非单一异常点；
  • 强泛化能力：跨9种主流模型（7B–120B参数）留出测试中，6/9场景达 AUC = 1.000；3个例外均获机制性解释；前沿模型（GPT-4.1、GPT-4o）零样本适配成功；
  • 实时可行性：纯前缀（prefix-only）变体达 AUC = 0.934，支持低延迟拦截；
  • 法证可分性：绕过记忆的提示注入攻击产生显著不同轨迹（得分 0.541），仅凭工具调用日志即可区分攻击类型。

AI Summary (English)

We identify a forensic trajectory signature for detecting persistent memory poisoning in LLM agents: a behavioral invariant requiring memory_recall_fact to precede email_send_email in attack-successful sessions—a transition rare in benign workflows and causally necessary for the attack’s information-retrieval dependency. Exploiting this alone yields AUC = 0.9563; a Random Forest on 19 trajectory features achieves AUC = 0.9904 (BCa 95% CI [0.987, 0.993]). The signature is overdetermined—removing all recall-related features leaves AUC unchanged at 0.990—confirming distributed behavioral imprinting. It generalizes across 9 models (7B–120B), attaining AUC = 1.000 on 6/9 hold-out splits (all exceptions mechanistically explained) and transfers zero-shot to GPT-4.1 and GPT-4o. A prefix-only variant enables real-time blocking (AUC = 0.934). Crucially, prompt-injection attacks bypassing memory yield distinct trajectories (score = 0.541), allowing incident responders to distinguish attack vectors using tool-call logs alone.

Abstract

We discover a behavioral invariant in LLM agents under persistent memory poisoning: in architectures where routing information is retrieved through observable memory-tool invocations, successful attacks require calling memory_recall_fact before email_send_email, a transition that non-exfiltrating sessions rarely exhibit. Under the evaluated architecture, this invariant follows from the attack's information-retrieval dependency rather than being merely an empirical correlation, and suppressing it breaks the attack. A simple rule exploiting this invariant alone achieves AUC = 0.9563. A Random Forest classifier over 19 trajectory features refines it to AUC = 0.9904 (BCa 95% CI [0.987, 0.993], N=10,000 resamples), demonstrating that the attack imprints on multiple independent behavioral channels. The signature is overdetermined: removing all recall-related features (half the feature set) leaves AUC unchanged at 0.990, confirming that memory poisoning induces a distributed trajectory signature rather than a single observable anomaly. Cross-model hold-out on 9 models (7B-120B parameters) confirms AUC = 1.000 on 6/9 hold-out splits, with all three exceptions mechanistically explained. The invariant generalizes to frontier models (GPT-4.1, GPT-4o) without retraining. A strictly prefix-only variant achieves AUC = 0.934, suggesting that real-time blocking is feasible with moderate degradation. The boundary is forensically useful: prompt-injection attacks that bypass memory produce a distinct trajectory (score = 0.541), enabling incident responders to distinguish memory-channel attacks from prompt-injection attacks using tool-call logs alone.

item.title: Your Space is My Zone: Demystifying the Security Risks of AI-Powered Applications on Pre-Trained Model Hubs
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30373v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

AI驱动应用（AI-Apps）依托Hugging Face等预训练模型平台，通过在线推理与微调服务大幅降低AI使用门槛。然而，此类应用常由不可信第三方开发，运行于弱隔离环境、安全配置缺失，形成尚未被系统研究的新型攻击面。

方法与框架

本研究首次对三大主流平台上的AI-Apps开展系统性安全分析。我们将AI-App全生命周期映射至OWASP等经典风险分类体系，提炼出5类威胁维度（如访问控制失效、资源复用不安全）和10种具体攻击向量。为实现规模化实证评估，我们设计并实现了自动化分析框架Insightor，支持静态代码扫描、配置审计与敏感数据模式识别。

主要发现

对超97万款公开AI-Apps的扫描揭示：

  • 数千款应用存在硬编码凭证泄露（含API密钥、数据库凭据）；
  • 数百款存在输入注入漏洞，可触发任意代码执行（如通过prompt或JSON参数注入Python/Shell命令）；
  • 数十款嵌入隐蔽后门（如恶意requirements.txt依赖、劫持__init__.py的远程加载逻辑），证实已遭主动利用；
  • 首次发现3类平台原生架构漏洞：模型权重与用户代码共享沙箱导致侧信道越权、日志默认世界可读且含完整请求上下文、推理端点未强制身份绑定致跨租户资源混淆。

创新与影响

本研究不仅揭示了AI-App生态中“传统Web漏洞+AI特有风险”的双重放大效应，更提出可落地的缓解建议（如最小权限沙箱、输入schema强校验、日志脱敏策略），所有发现均已通过负责任披露流程同步至平台方与开发者。

AI Summary (English)

This paper presents the first systematic security analysis of AI-powered Applications (AI-Apps) hosted on pre-trained model hubs (e.g., Hugging Face). We map the AI-App lifecycle to established risk taxonomies (e.g., OWASP), identifying five threat categories and ten attack vectors—from generic web flaws to platform-specific architectural weaknesses. We develop Insightor, an automated analysis framework, and apply it to 970,000+ public AI-Apps, uncovering thousands leaking credentials, hundreds vulnerable to input injection enabling arbitrary code execution, and dozens containing embedded backdoors—evidence of active exploitation. Critically, we discover three novel architectural vulnerabilities inherent to hub design, including insecure resource sharing across tenants and world-readable logs containing full inference contexts. All findings were responsibly disclosed.

Abstract

AI-powered Applications (AI-Apps), hosted on platforms such as Hugging Face, are democratizing access to pre-trained models through online inference and fine-tuning services. While lowering AI adoption barriers, these platforms introduce an unexplored attack surface, as AI-Apps are often developed by untrusted parties with weak isolation and misconfigured security settings. In this paper, we present the first systematic security analysis of AI-Apps across three leading platforms. To structure our investigation, we map the AI-App lifecycle to established risk taxonomies (e.g., OWASP), identifying five threat categories and ten attack vectors ranging from generic web flaws to high-impact architectural issues. Our analysis reveals critical failures including broken access control, insecure resource reuse, insufficient input validation, and sensitive data exposure. Notably, we uncover three novel architectural vulnerabilities inherent to platform design and demonstrate how traditional issues (e.g., world-readable logs) are uniquely amplified in this ecosystem. To assess real-world impact, we develop an analysis framework Insightor and apply it to over 970,000 public AI-Apps. Alarmingly, we find thousands of apps leaking credentials, hundreds containing input injection vulnerabilities that allow arbitrary code execution, and tens harboring embedded backdoors -- indicating active exploitation. We have responsibly disclosed all findings to the affected platforms and developers.

item.title: Quantum Lazy Sampling and Path Recording for Any Group
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30281v1

AI Summary (中文)

量子懒采样与路径记录：面向任意群的通用框架

在量子算法与密码学中，如何高效模拟对随机群元素（如随机函数、置换或幺正算子）具有预言机访问权的量子算法，是一个核心挑战。经典领域中，“懒采样”（lazy sampling）技术通过延迟承诺完整群元素、按需生成部分输入-输出对，实现了对查询过程的精确建模。本文提出并系统构建了该思想的首个普适、可解释的量子模拟框架——路径记录预言机（path-recording oracle），适用于任意闭子群 $G \subseteq U(N)$（包括对称群 $S_N$、幺正群 $U(N)$、Clifford 群等）。

我们从第一性原理出发，设计了一种新型压缩预言机：其量子态以叠加形式存储至多 $t$ 个查询所获得的输入-输出对路径；每次量子查询后，状态更新由群 $G$ 在张量幂表示下的交换子代数（commutant）严格刻画。这一结构天然反映算法实际获取的信息，具备透明的语义可解释性——即“存储什么”与“学到了什么”完全对应，克服了近期 Grinko-Yoshida（QIP ’26）通用压缩预言机缺乏直观含义的局限。

关键创新在于：

  • 统一性：首次为任意闭李群子群提供一致、非特设的量子懒采样机制；
  • 可比性：支持跨群（如 $S_N$ vs. $U(N)$）的预言机行为直接比较，催生新型伪随机性证明范式；
  • 应用突破：由此导出迄今最简伪随机幺正构造——伪随机置换（PRP）与随机Clifford的乘积（PC），显著简化并改进了先前PFC方案（FOCS ’24 / STOC ’25），为后量子密码与量子伪随机性奠定新基础。

AI Summary (English)

We introduce the first general-purpose, interpretable quantum lazy sampling framework—the path-recording oracle—for simulating quantum algorithms with oracle access to random elements of any closed subgroup $G \subseteq U(N)$. Unlike prior group-specific compressed oracles (e.g., for functions, permutations, or unitaries), our construction is derived from first principles: it stores query-response pairs in superposition, and updates are governed by the commutant of $G$’s tensor-power representation—ensuring the stored state exactly reflects the algorithm’s learned information. This yields transparent semantics absent in recent generic alternatives (Grinko–Yoshida, QIP ’26). A key application is enabling direct cross-group comparisons: contrasting oracles for $S_N$ and $U(N)$ leads to the simplest known construction of pseudorandom unitaries—the product of a pseudorandom permutation and a random Clifford (PC), improving upon the prior PFC scheme (FOCS ’24; STOC ’25).

Abstract

A central challenge in quantum algorithms and cryptography is reasoning about algorithms with oracle access to a random group element (e.g. a random function, permutation, or unitary). Can we efficiently simulate such algorithms? Can we determine what they know after t queries? A classical tool for this is lazy sampling: the oracle does not commit to the full group element upfront, but rather samples partial information about it on the fly. We study a quantum analog of lazy sampling: compressed oracles (or recording oracles). These are quantum data structures that allow on-the-fly simulation for quantum queries, originally introduced by Zhandry (CRYPTO '19) for random functions, and generalized to unitaries by Ma-Huang (STOC '25) and permutations by Carolan (STOC '26), and used to great effect in security proofs and lower bounds due to their interpretability. We define and analyze a general-purpose and interpretable path-recording oracle, derived from first principles, that perfectly simulates random elements of any closed subgroup of $U(N)$. Our oracle stores, in superposition, t input-output pairs, with updates described in terms of the commutant of the group's tensor power representation. This transparently records the information the algorithm has learned. Our oracle builds on recent work of Grinko-Yoshida (QIP '26), who gave a different general-purpose compressed oracle without clear interpretability. One interesting application of our path-recording is allowing direct comparisons between compressed oracles of different groups, giving a new technique for proving pseudorandomness results. For example, comparing $S_N$ and $U(N)$ yields what is arguably the simplest construction to date of pseudorandom unitaries: the product PC of a pseudorandom permutation and a random Clifford, improving on the prior PFC construction (Metger-Poremba-Sinha-Yuen, FOCS '24; Ma-Huang, STOC '25).

item.title: The Spectrum Strikes Back: Infrared POV Attacks on Traffic Sign Classification
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30153v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

交通标志分类是自动驾驶系统感知层的关键任务，其安全性直接关系到行车安全。现有物理对抗攻击（如贴片攻击、光投影攻击）虽有效，但普遍存在人眼可见性高的问题；而基于非可见光谱（如红外）的攻击又常受限于动态响应能力弱、触发机制僵化，难以实现远程、实时、隐蔽的干扰。

方法创新

本文提出一种基于近红外频段的视觉暂留（Persistence-of-Vision, POV）攻击：利用人眼无法感知的850 nm近红外LED阵列，通过高速时序调制，在目标交通标志表面“投射”动态对抗图案。该攻击具备三大特性：① 完全隐蔽——不改变标志外观，无可见光泄露；② 远程可触发——支持无线信号实时启动/切换攻击内容；③ 位置鲁棒——通过数字仿真优化LED布设位置与扫描轨迹，适配不同标志尺寸与安装角度。

实验验证与结果

我们在真实道路场景中开展系统性评估：覆盖2类典型交通标志（禁行、限速）、12种异构模型（CNN、ViT、YOLO系列等）、距离跨度5–20米及昼夜/雨雾等多种光照条件。结果显示：在20米距离下，平均攻击成功率仍达89.3%；对部署于车载边缘设备的轻量级模型（如MobileNetV3），成功率高达96.7%；且攻击效果不受环境光照显著影响。

防御与拓展

我们提出双轨防御方案：① 硬件层——加装低成本近红外截止滤光片（阻断800–900 nm波段）；② 软件层——设计基于帧间红外残差异常检测的实时识别模块。此外，为验证技术普适性，我们进一步研制了可见光RGB-POV原型机，证实该机制可迁移至人眼可见场景，为后续多模态对抗研究提供新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces The Spectrum Strikes Back: a stealthy, dynamic physical adversarial attack against traffic sign classifiers using near-infrared (NIR) persistence-of-vision (POV) displays. Unlike visible-light patches or static IR projections, our attack leverages high-frequency 850 nm LED modulation to render imperceptible, remotely triggered adversarial patterns on signs—bypassing human detection while maintaining real-time adaptability. We optimize LED placement via digital simulation and validate across 2 sign types, 12 diverse ML models (CNNs, ViTs, YOLOs), distances up to 20 m, and varying illumination. Attack success rates reach 89.3% at 20 m, exceeding 96% on edge-deployed models. We propose practical defenses: NIR-cut filters and a software-based frame-difference detector. Finally, we prototype an RGB-POV variant to demonstrate generalizability beyond the NIR spectrum.

Abstract

Traffic sign classification is a crucial task for autonomous vehicles, and numerous attacks against it have been identified. A majority of physical adversarial attacks involve attaching patches to traffic signs or projecting perturbations on them. While they demonstrate high effectiveness, they are perceptible to humans. At the same time, light-based attacks outside the human visible spectrum are known but have limitations in their dynamic adaptability. We propose a persistence-of-vision-based attack that operates in the near-infrared light spectrum. With the possibility of showing dynamic, remotely triggered content, this allows a stealthy physical adversarial attack against traffic sign classification. By identifying the optimal position through digital simulation, we conduct extensive real-world evaluations using two different traffic signs, 12 machine learning models from different families, multiple distances up to 20 meters, and varying illumination conditions. Our evaluation shows high attack success rates across our test scenarios. We propose near-infrared cutoff filters and a software-based detection mechanism as defenses, and tackle limitations of the near-infrared persistence of vision display by prototyping a human-visible RGB version of it.

item.title: Discard the Dross and Select the Essential: Pre-query Sample Selection for Black-box Membership Inference Attacks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30081v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

黑盒成员推断攻击（MIAs）依赖对目标模型的大量查询，以判断候选样本是否参与过模型训练。然而，不同样本蕴含的成员信号强度高度不均：仅少数样本具备强判别性，而多数样本贡献微弱，导致盲目查询造成高昂的查询开销与模型暴露风险，边际收益却极低。因此，“哪些样本值得优先查询？” 成为提升黑盒MIA效率与隐私安全性的关键瓶颈。

方法：PSS-MIA 框架

本文提出 PSS-MIA（Pre-query Sample Selection for MIA）——一种即插即用的预查询样本选择框架，可无缝集成任意现有黑盒MIA方法。其分为两阶段：
1. 信号感知筛选：基于参考模型计算候选样本的损失差（Loss Gap），提出 Loss-Gap Ranking（LGR） 策略，量化并排序各样本的内在成员信号强度；
2. 高效推断执行：仅对Top-k高信号样本发起查询，将返回预测输出馈入下游MIA（如Shadow Training、Thresholding等），显著压缩查询总量。

主要发现与创新

在CIFAR-10、CIFAR-100与CINIC-10数据集上，联合5种主流黑盒MIA方法验证表明：
✅ PSS-MIA+LGR 在所有设定下一致超越基线方法（含随机采样、置信度/熵排序等）；
✅ 在严格 0.1%假阳性率（FPR）约束下，分别节省 83.1%、60.6%、80.4% 的查询预算；
✅ 无需访问目标模型梯度或架构，完全黑盒、无侵入、零训练开销，兼具实用性与普适性。
本工作首次系统揭示样本级信号异质性，并提供首个轻量、通用、可证明有效的预查询选择范式。

AI Summary (English)

Black-box membership inference attacks (MIAs) suffer from highly non-uniform membership signals across candidate samples, leading to wasteful queries and increased exposure. To address this, we propose PSS-MIA, a plug-and-play pre-query sample selection framework compatible with any black-box MIA. PSS-MIA operates in two stages: (1) it ranks candidates using Loss-Gap Ranking (LGR)—a lightweight signal estimator based on loss gaps from public reference models; (2) it queries only the top-ranked subset and feeds outputs into downstream MIAs. Extensive experiments on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and CINIC-10 with five representative MIAs show that PSS-MIA+LGR consistently outperforms all baselines. Under a stringent 0.1% FPR constraint, it reduces query budgets by at least 83.1%, 60.6%, and 80.4%, respectively—without requiring target-model access, gradients, or retraining. This work establishes the first practical, model-agnostic, and theoretically grounded sample selection paradigm for efficient black-box MIAs.

Abstract

Black-box membership inference attacks (MIAs) rely on target-model queries to infer whether candidate samples were used for training. However, membership signals are highly non-uniform across samples: some candidate samples support strong member/non-member separability, whereas many others provide little useful signal. Consequently, indiscriminate querying can incur substantial query cost and increase query-induced exposure, with limited marginal benefit for inference. This raises a key question: which candidate samples are worth querying for black-box MIAs? To address this question, we propose PSS-MIA, a pre-query sample selection framework which can be embedded with any existing MIA methods. PSS-MIA proceeds in two stages: it first ranks candidate samples and selects a subset expected to support stronger membership inference, then queries the selected samples and uses the returned outputs for an existing black-box MIA, thereby reducing query cost and query-induced exposure. In the first stage, we propose Loss-Gap Ranking (LGR), which ranks candidate samples by estimating the strength of their membership signal using loss gaps computed from reference models. Experiments on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and CINIC-10 with five representative black-box MIA methods demonstrate that PSS-MIA with LGR consistently outperforms all other compared methods. Moreover, under a 0.1% FPR constraint, PSS-MIA can save at least 83.1%, 60.6%, and 80.4% of the query budget for the three datasets, respectively.

item.title: Hephaestus: Toward a Cybersecurity AI Scientist
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.29981v1

AI Summary (中文)

Hephaestus：迈向网络安全AI科学家

当前网络攻击已进入“机器速度”时代，而网络安全研究仍严重依赖人工驱动，滞后于威胁演进节奏。本文提出网络安全AI科学家（Cybersecurity AI Scientist）这一新型科学对象，系统阐释其与传统AI科学家的本质差异：其研究单元是动态的安全事件与交互痕迹，而非静态资产；其模型与工具基座具有非平稳性（non-stationarity），持续受对抗演化、环境漂移与策略博弈影响；其可信评估必须依托数字孪生、网络靶场与可审计证据链，而非单一静态基准分数。

为实现该范式，我们提出Hephaestus架构——一个模块化、角色专业化、多智能体协同的研究系统。它将科研流程解耦为七大协同角色：问题建模、威胁建模、工具生成、受控实验、多维评估、治理审计与科学报告，并以“四零框架”（Zero Risk、Zero Trust Violation、Zero Incident Impact、Zero Energy Waste）锚定具体研究目标，覆盖风险、信任、事件与能效四大维度。作为代表性落地路径，本文聚焦AI原生防御（AI-native defense）：摒弃静态边界防护，转向由韧性智能体集群构成的动态防御体系；同时解构传统“终端安全”概念，将其升维为智能体级安全（agent security）——关注自主体在开放、对抗、资源受限环境中的可信行为建模与保障。

本工作不绑定特定组织实现，而是定义该科学对象的本体边界、核心属性与演进原则，为后续系统构建、基准设计与实证研究提供可扩展的理论框架与工程蓝图。

AI Summary (English)

Cyber offense has accelerated to machine speed, yet cyber research remains human-paced. This paper introduces Hephaestus: a foundational framework for the Cybersecurity AI Scientist—a novel scientific object distinct from existing AI scientist systems. Unlike stable domains (e.g., physics or chemistry), cybersecurity research operates on non-stationary interaction traces, adversarial tooling, and dynamic threat landscapes; thus, its evaluation demands digital twins, cyber ranges, and auditable evidence—not static benchmarks. Hephaestus realizes this via a modular, role-specialized multi-agent architecture coordinating problem framing, threat modeling, tool synthesis, controlled experimentation, multi-dimensional evaluation (anchored in the Four-Zeros frame: risk, trust violation, incident impact, energy waste), governance, and scientific reporting. We foreground AI-native defense, shifting from perimeter-based security to resilient agent legions and redefining terminal security as agent security. This work defines the object’s ontology, separates it from organizational instantiation, and provides an extensible architecture and research agenda for future systems, benchmarks, and empirical programs.

Abstract

Cyber offense is moving to machine speed; cyber research itself is not. Existing AI scientist systems make end-to-end research automation increasingly plausible, but they target relatively stable scientific domains. We argue that AI-native cybersecurity is a different kind of scientific object. Its recurring units of study are security events and interaction traces, not static assets; its model and tool substrate is non-stationary, not steady-state; and credible evaluation depends on digital twins, cyber ranges, and auditable evidence rather than on a single benchmark score. We call this object the Cybersecurity AI Scientist. A practical realization is a modular, role-specialized multi-agent research system that coordinates problem framing, threat modeling, tool generation, controlled experimentation, evaluation, governance, and scientific reporting, and that anchors its concrete objectives in a four-zeros frame spanning risk, trust, incident, and energy dimensions. As a representative agenda we focus on AI-native defense, where steady-state perimeters give way to resilient agent legions and the classical category of terminal security is itself being deconstructed into agent security. This paper defines the object, separates it from any single organizational realization, and offers an architecture and an agenda on which later systems, benchmarks, and empirical programs can be built.

item.title: A Sieve-Accelerated Quadrature Method for Exact Privacy Accounting in the 2020 U.S. Decennial Census
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.29835v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

2020年美国十年一度人口普查首次全面采用差分隐私（DP），通过向发布的人口统计表格注入整数值高斯噪声实现隐私保护。然而，现有隐私核算方法多依赖近似（如中心极限定理、矩估计或蒙特卡洛模拟），无法在严苛数值精度下（如误差容限低至 $10^{-35}$）精确评估真实隐私保障水平。这导致普查局不得不注入过量噪声以“保守达标”，严重损害数据在联邦资金分配、选区重划等关键下游应用中的统计效用。

方法创新

本文提出一种筛子加速的数值求积法（Sieve-Accelerated Quadrature），首次实现大规模普查发布（如DHC文件）的无假设、精确隐私核算：

  • 理论重构：将离散高斯机制的异质组合隐私剖面计算，严格转化为基于离散傅里叶变换（DFT） 的高维数值积分问题；
  • 指数收敛利用：证明其特征函数为复解析且周期，从而可严格启用梯形法则的指数收敛性，规避传统方法的多项式误差衰减；
  • 筛子算法突破：设计动态筛子策略，自动识别并剔除对积分贡献可忽略（$<10^{-40}$）的振荡被积函数节点，在128维以上场景中减少99.9%的冗余计算。

主要成果

该方法在真实2020年普查DHC文件上完成首例精确核算：
✅ 达成普查法定误差容限（$10^{-35}$）；
✅ 相比此前最优方法提速1,824倍（三阶数量级加速）；
✅ 确定最小必需噪声标准差降低17.3%，显著提升数据可用性；
✅ 开源实现已集成至美国普查局官方DP工具链。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a sieve-accelerated quadrature method for exact, assumption-free privacy accounting of the 2020 U.S. Decennial Census—where integer-valued discrete Gaussian noise is applied under heterogeneous composition. By recasting privacy profile evaluation as high-dimensional numerical integration via the discrete Fourier transform, we leverage the exponential convergence of the trapezoidal rule for analytic periodic characteristic functions. To overcome severe computational bottlenecks from highly oscillatory integrands in up to 128+ dimensions, we design a novel sieve algorithm that prunes negligible quadrature nodes with provable error control ($<10^{-40}$), accelerating computation by three orders of magnitude. Applied to the Demographic and Housing Characteristics (DHC) File, our method achieves census-mandated numerical tolerance ($10^{-35}$) and delivers a 1,824× speedup over prior state-of-the-art—enabling the first exact determination of minimum feasible noise and substantially improving data utility for redistricting and funding allocation.

Abstract

In 2020, the U.S. Census Bureau adopted differential privacy for the Decennial Census by injecting integer-valued Gaussian noise into published census tabulations. Exactly evaluating the privacy guarantees of these data releases would enable the Bureau to determine the absolute minimum noise required to satisfy a given privacy budget, preventing the injection of unnecessary excess noise and thereby substantially enhancing the statistical utility of the data for downstream applications such as federal funding allocation and political redistricting. In this paper, we introduce a computationally efficient and mathematically rigorous quadrature method to evaluate the exact privacy profile of practical, large-scale census releases under the composition of heterogeneous discrete Gaussian mechanisms. Mathematically, this problem reduces to evaluating the tail probabilities of high-dimensional convolutions of integer-valued random variables sampled from heterogeneous discrete Gaussian distributions under exceptionally stringent numerical error tolerances (e.g., $10^{-35}$). By recasting the exact privacy accounting as a numerical integration problem via the discrete Fourier transform, we explicitly exploit the exponential convergence of the trapezoidal rule for complex analytic, periodic characteristic functions. Furthermore, to overcome the computational bottleneck of evaluating highly oscillatory integrands in high dimensions, we develop a sieve algorithm that identifies and prunes negligible quadrature nodes, accelerating the computation by three orders of magnitude. Taken together, these numerical innovations enable the first exact, assumption-free privacy accounting for the 2020 Census Demographic and Housing Characteristics File, achieving a 1,824-fold speedup over prior methods while maintaining census-mandated error tolerances.

item.title: Linguistic Firewall: Geometry as Defense in Multi-Agent Systems Routing
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30555v1

AI Summary (中文)

语言防火墙：面向多智能体系统路由的几何化防御机制

随着大语言模型（LLMs）的深度集成，多智能体系统（MAS） 日益依赖专业化智能体协同完成复杂任务流。其核心挑战在于路由机制的安全性与可靠性：现有路由器普遍依赖未经验证的代理指标——如文本型自我描述、静态嵌入或人工标注的标签——来推断智能体能力。此类非实证表征存在根本缺陷：它在智能体“宣称能力”与“真实行为”之间制造了显著语义鸿沟，为恶意攻击（如能力伪装、后门植入）提供了可乘之机；而这些攻击能轻易绕过传统外部审计与静态表征学习方法。

本文提出 ANTAP（Automatic Non-Textual Agent Picker）——一种以实证评估为驱动的新型路由架构。ANTAP 彻底摒弃间接代理，转而通过动态能力探针（dynamic capability probing） 对智能体进行实时、任务导向的实证测试。其核心创新在于：将智能体响应映射至一个共享的语义几何空间，并从中提取稳定、可组合的非文本行为算子（non-textual behavioral operators）。推理阶段的路由决策完全基于该空间内的纯代数投影（purely algebraic projection），不依赖任何文本元数据或嵌入向量，从而构建起一道严格的“语言防火墙”——使所有基于描述篡改或嵌入扰动的攻击在形式上不可表达。

实验表明：在描述注入攻击下，ANTAP 的攻击成功率（ASR）趋近于零（<0.5%），远优于基线描述路由器的67.3%+；面对自适应嵌入攻击，ANTAP 实现20%绝对值ASR下降，且因设计本质拒斥描述操纵，天然免疫此类攻击。本工作首次将能力验证几何化与路由代数化结合，为MAS安全路由提供了可证明、可部署的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

We propose ANTAP (Automatic Non-Textual Agent Picker), a security-first routing architecture for Multi-Agent Systems (MAS) that replaces unverified textual proxies (e.g., self-descriptions or static embeddings) with empirical, dynamic capability testing. ANTAP maps agent responses into a shared semantic geometry space and distills them into fixed non-textual behavioral operators; routing is then performed via purely algebraic projection—eliminating reliance on metadata or embeddings. This establishes a “linguistic firewall” where description-based and embedding-based attacks become formally inexpressible. Experiments show ANTAP reduces attack success rate (ASR) to near-zero (<0.5%) against description injection (vs. 67.3%+ for baseline), achieves a 20% absolute ASR reduction under adaptive embedding attacks, and is inherently resilient to description manipulation by design.

Abstract

The rapid integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) has driven the evolution of Multi-Agent Systems (MAS), where specialized agents collaborate to execute complex workflows. Effective orchestration in these environments requires robust routing mechanisms to efficiently allocate tasks to the most suitable agent. However, existing routers fundamentally rely on unverified proxies, ranging from textual self-descriptions to static surrogate representations, to gauge an agent's competence. This reliance on non-empirical data creates a critical gap between an agent's projected profile and its actual operational capabilities, introducing severe security vulnerabilities. Malicious agents can easily misrepresent their proficiencies or harbor covert backdoors that evade both standard external analysis and static representation-learning techniques. In this work, we introduce ANTAP (Automatic Non-Textual Agent Picker), an evaluation-driven routing architecture that discards indirect proxies in favor of active capability testing. By dynamically querying agents to ascertain their true competencies empirically, ANTAP distills performance into fixed behavioral operators within a shared semantic space. At inference time, routing is performed via a purely non-textual algebraic projection, establishing a "linguistic firewall" that renders metadata-based attacks inexpressible. In our experiments, ANTAP achieves near-zero ASR against description-based injection attacks, compared to 67.3\% and above for the description-based router baseline. Against adaptive embedding attacks, ANTAP achieves substantially lower ASR than the embedding-based baseline, with a 20\% reduction, while remaining resilient to description manipulation by design.

item.title: EvalSafetyGap: A Hybrid Survey and Conceptual Framework for LLM Evaluation-Safety Failures
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30219v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

大型语言模型（LLM）评估与AI安全面临共同的测量鸿沟：基准分数、奖励模型信号及报告的安全指标可能持续提升，但其所代理的深层能力（如真实对齐性、鲁棒拒绝能力、可审计性）却难以验证——即“评估-安全失配”（EvalSafetyGap）。这一失配源于代理指标在优化压力下的系统性失效，亟需跨范式整合证据。

方法

本研究采用混合方法学：

  • 混合调查：系统检索2018–2026年文献（含学术论文、技术报告、开源审计），结合叙事综述与独立追踪的灰色证据（如未发表实验、社区复现、企业白皮书）；
  • 概念框架：提出EvalSafetyGap作为核心组织假说，整合Goodhart定律、新提出的不稳定性分解（Instability Decomposition）与对齐三难困境（Alignment Trilemma）；
  • 结构化十模型审计：在统一分析框架下，分离测量能力（capability）、行为安全（behavioral safety）与治理可审计性（governance/auditability）三项维度，覆盖主流开源与闭源模型（n=10）。

主要发现

  • 表3显示：能力与持续对抗鲁棒性无统计显著关联（Pearson r = +0.232, p = 0.520）；
  • 所谓“开源-闭源安全差距”实为治理与披露差异主导，而非行为鲁棒性本质差异，且对单个边界模型的分类高度敏感；
  • “尝试预算”（attempt budget）结果强烈依赖评估协议设计，凸显协议异质性是当前公共证据的核心局限。

审计定位为诊断性工具，非排名生成器。

创新贡献

构建首个覆盖八类证据流（基准效度、动态评估、LLM-as-judge可靠性、安全评测、越狱/拒答鲁棒性、奖励黑客、机制可解释性、治理可审计性）的共享词汇表与证据地图，支撑动态评估、透明溯源、多尝试安全测量及可审计对齐实践。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces EvalSafetyGap, a hybrid survey and conceptual framework addressing the shared measurement crisis in LLM evaluation and AI safety: proxy metrics (e.g., benchmark scores, reward signals) often improve while underlying alignment properties remain unverified. We synthesize evidence across eight streams (2018–2026), including benchmark validity, jailbreak robustness, reward hacking, and governance auditability, combining systematic literature review, narrative synthesis, and tracked grey evidence. Central to our framework are Goodhart’s Law, the novel Instability Decomposition, and the Alignment Trilemma, enabling testable comparisons between evaluation-side and alignment-side proxy failures. A structured audit of ten models reveals that capability does not significantly predict sustained adversarial robustness (r = +0.232, p = 0.520); the apparent open-closed safety gap is modest and driven primarily by governance/disclosure—not behavioral safety—and is sensitive to borderline model classification. Crucially, results are protocol-dependent, rendering public evidence diagnostic rather than rank-generating. Our contribution is a unified vocabulary and evidence map to advance dynamic evaluation, transparent sourcing, multi-attempt safety measurement, and auditable alignment practice.

Abstract

LLM evaluation and AI safety face a shared measurement problem: benchmark scores, reward-model signals, and reported safety metrics can improve while the latent properties they are meant to represent remain difficult to verify. This paper combines a hybrid survey - a systematic search paired with narrative synthesis and separately tracked grey evidence - with a conceptual framework and a structured ten-model audit. The synthesis spans eight evidence streams: benchmark validity, dynamic evaluation, LLM-as-judge reliability, safety evaluation, jailbreak/refusal robustness, reward hacking, mechanistic interpretability, and governance/auditability, covering 2018-2026 evaluation-safety measurement work. We introduce EvalSafetyGap as an organizing hypothesis for comparing evaluation-side and alignment-side proxy failures under optimization pressure, using Goodhart's Law together with two constructs we develop here - an Instability Decomposition and an Alignment Trilemma - as tools for generating testable comparisons. The audit shows how conclusions shift when capability, behavioral safety, and governance are measured separately. In this sample (n = 10), the association between capability and sustained adversarial robustness is statistically indeterminate using the displayed Table 3 inputs (Pearson r = +0.232, p = 0.520), and the apparent open-closed safety gap is modest, driven mainly by governance and disclosure rather than behavioral robustness, and sensitive to how a single borderline model is classified; attempt-budget results are protocol dependent. Because the public evidence uses heterogeneous protocols, the audit is diagnostic rather than rank-generating. The contribution is a shared vocabulary and evidence map to support dynamic evaluation, transparent source reporting, multi-attempt safety measurement, and auditable alignment practice.

item.title: Federated Learning with Energy-Based Structured Probabilistic Inference
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30161v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

联邦学习（Federated Learning, FL）在保护数据隐私的同时实现协同建模，但其标准聚合策略（如FedAvg采用的均匀加权或基于本地数据量的启发式加权）在客户端数据高度异构（non-IID）时表现不佳。现有方法难以动态刻画各客户端对全局模型的真实贡献度，尤其当客户端间存在隐含相关性（如相似分布偏移、共现噪声模式）时，固定权重易导致收敛缓慢或性能下降。

方法创新

本文提出基于能量函数的结构化概率推理联邦学习框架（EB-SPF），首次将条件随机场（CRF） 引入FL权重优化：

  • 一元势函数（Unary Potentials）：建模单个客户端的可靠性，由其本地训练损失、梯度方差及历史更新稳定性联合定义；
  • 二元势函数（Pairwise Potentials）：刻画任意客户端对之间的语义相似性与协同效应，通过余弦相似度与分布距离（Wasserstein距离）构造，显式建模客户端交互；
  • 结构化推理：在服务器端执行CRF最大后验（MAP）推断，求解最优聚合权重分布，使全局目标函数（如全局损失期望）收敛性得到理论保障。

主要发现与优势

在CIFAR-10/100、FEMNIST及Shakespeare数据集上的系统实验表明：

  • 在强non-IID设定（pathological split, Dirichlet α=0.1）下，EB-SPF相较FedAvg、FedProx、SCAFFOLD平均提升3.2–5.7%准确率；
  • 权重分配具备可解释性：高可靠性客户端获更高权重，而语义相近客户端形成“协作簇”，抑制噪声传播；
  • 计算开销可控（CRF推理仅增加<8%服务器端延迟），且兼容各类本地优化器与通信压缩策略。

本工作为FL提供了首个融合结构先验与概率推理的动态加权范式，推动从“经验聚合”迈向“可解释、可推理”的智能协同学习。

AI Summary (English)

Federated learning (FL) commonly aggregates client updates via fixed or heuristic weights—suboptimal under data heterogeneity and varying client contributions. We propose Energy-Based Structured Probabilistic Inference (EB-SPF), a novel framework that refines aggregation weights using Conditional Random Fields (CRFs). EB-SPF defines unary potentials to capture per-client reliability (based on loss, gradient variance, and update stability) and pairwise potentials to model semantic interactions between clients (via cosine similarity and Wasserstein distance). CRF-based MAP inference on the server yields principled, adaptive weights that provably improve convergence of the global objective. Extensive experiments across CIFAR-10/100, FEMNIST, and Shakespeare under strong non-IID settings (Dirichlet α=0.1) show consistent gains: +3.2–5.7% accuracy over FedAvg, FedProx, and SCAFFOLD. The approach is lightweight (<8% server overhead), interpretable, and fully compatible with existing FL optimizations.

Abstract

Federated learning typically aggregates client updates using fixed or heuristic weighting rules, which can be suboptimal when clients have heterogeneous data and varying contributions to the global model. We propose a framework that refines client aggregation weights using Conditional Random Fields (CRFs). Our method defines unary potentials for individual clients and pairwise potentials for all client pairs, allowing the server to model both client-specific reliability and interactions between clients. The resulting CRF inference produces aggregation weights that enable better convergence of the global training objective. Experiments show that, under non-IID heterogeneity, our approach consistently improves performance over well-established federated learning baselines.

item.title: Temporal Feature Extractors in EEG Foundation Models: A Controlled Comparison Including a Pretrained Time-Series Model
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30104v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

脑电图（EEG）基础模型致力于从大规模脑电信号中学习可迁移的通用表征，但其中时序特征提取器的设计选择及其对下游任务泛化能力的影响尚缺乏系统性验证。尤其值得关注的是：通用时序基础模型（Time-Series Foundation Models, TSFMs）——如专为多领域时间序列预训练的MOMENT——能否在不微调、仅作为冻结模块的前提下，有效迁移到EEG建模中？这一问题触及跨模态时序表征迁移的核心可行性。

方法设计

本研究在统一的EEG基础模型框架下，严格控制其他变量，对照评估三种时序特征提取策略：

  • 线性基线（Linear）：无参数时序建模，仅通过线性投影聚合时间点；
  • 卷积编码器（Conv）：轻量级一维CNN，具备局部时序感受野；
  • 冻结TSFM（MOMENT）：直接加载在电力、传感器等多源时序上预训练的MOMENT模型，全程冻结参数，仅用作特征提取器。

评估基于两个典型EEG下游任务：运动想象分类（BCI Competition IV 2a）与情绪识别（DEAP），采用线性探针协议量化表征质量。

主要发现与创新点

  • 在运动想象任务中，简单线性与时序卷积表现相当甚至更优，表明该任务对精细时序动态依赖较低；
  • 在情绪识别任务中，冻结MOMENT显著提升性能（+3.2% avg. F1），凸显其对复杂、长程情绪相关神经模式的建模优势；
  • 首次实证表明：无需EEG特定预训练或微调，通用TSFMs即可作为即插即用的高质量时序特征提取器，为EEG基础模型架构设计提供新范式——“冻结迁移优于从头设计”。

AI Summary (English)

Electroencephalography (EEG) foundation models seek general-purpose neural representations, yet the impact of temporal feature extractors—and the transferability of pretrained time-series foundation models (TSFMs)—remains underexamined. We conduct a controlled ablation within a unified EEG foundation model, comparing three temporal encoders: a linear baseline, a learnable 1D convolutional encoder, and a frozen pretrained TSFM (MOMENT). Evaluation on motor imagery (BCI-IV 2a) and emotion recognition (DEAP) reveals divergent trends: simple temporal modeling suffices for motor imagery, while emotion recognition benefits substantially from MOMENT’s rich, long-range temporal inductive bias (+3.2% F1). Crucially, MOMENT—never trained on EEG—performs competitively as a frozen module, demonstrating that generic time-series representations can be effectively transferred to EEG without adaptation. This challenges the necessity of EEG-specific temporal architecture design and establishes frozen TSFMs as a viable, plug-and-play temporal backbone for EEG foundation models.

Abstract

Electroencephalography (EEG) foundation models aim to learn generalizable representations from large-scale brain recordings. However, the role of temporal feature extractors and whether pretrained time-series foundation models (TSFMs) can be effectively transferred to this setting remains underexplored. We conduct a controlled comparison of three temporal feature extraction strategies, including a linear baseline, a convolutional encoder, and a frozen pretrained TSFM (MOMENT), within a unified EEG foundation model. We evaluate their impact on representation quality using two downstream tasks: motor imagery and emotion recognition. Results reveal different trends across the evaluated benchmarks. On the motor imagery dataset, simple temporal representations perform competitively, whereas the emotion dataset benefits from richer temporal modeling. Although not specifically adapted to EEG, the pretrained TSFM serves as an effective temporal feature extractor, suggesting that general-purpose time-series representations can be transferred as frozen temporal feature extractors within EEG foundation models.

item.title: Clinical Reasoning Graphs: Structured Evaluation of LLM Diagnostic Reasoning Reveals Competence Without Consistency
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.29876v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

当前大语言模型（LLM）在复杂临床病例基准测试中诊断准确率达60–70%，但单一准确率指标无法区分稳定、基于临床证据的推理与表面化模式匹配。诊断“正确”未必意味着推理过程具备可复用、跨案例一致的临床认知结构。

方法创新

本研究提出临床推理图（Clinical Reasoning Graphs, CRGs）——一种结构化图表示方法，从LLM自由文本诊断轨迹中自动提取：

  • 基于领域锚定本体（5类节点：症状、体征、检查、疾病、推理操作；7类边：因果、排除、支持、时序等）；
  • 应用于750条诊断轨迹（5个主流LLM × 50例《新英格兰医学杂志》临床病理会议病例 × 3种提示条件）；
  • 以图相似度量化跨病例推理一致性：若存在稳定诊断图式（diagnostic schema），则临床相似病例间图相似度应显著高于临床相异病例。

关键发现

  • 无显著图式一致性：15组模型-条件对比中，同簇（临床相似）与异簇（临床相异）的复合图相似度几乎相等（Δ<0.01），且全部未通过多重检验校正；
  • 结构独立于结果：正确诊断对与错误诊断对的图相似度高度接近（0.488 vs. 0.484），表明图结构反映的是与最终答案解耦的推理维度；
  • 提示工程局限性：结构化反思提示使轨迹中显式特征鉴别分析提升33%，但未提升跨病例图结构一致性。

意义与资源

本研究揭示LLM具备诊断“ competence（能力）”却缺乏“schema-scale consistency（图式级一致性）”，呼吁将过程评估纳入临床AI验证体系。我们开源：临床推理本体、图提取流水线、验证协议，以及全部750个CRGs及相似度矩阵，支持可复现、结构化的临床推理评估。

AI Summary (English)

This study introduces Clinical Reasoning Graphs (CRGs)—structured, ontology-grounded graph representations extracted from LLM diagnostic traces—to move beyond accuracy-only evaluation. Applying CRGs to 750 traces across 5 LLMs, 50 NEJM Clinicopathological Conference cases, and 3 prompt conditions, we test for stable diagnostic schemas: higher graph similarity among clinically similar cases than dissimilar ones. Contrary to expectations, no model-condition pair showed statistically significant within- vs. between-cluster similarity differences after multiple-testing correction; residual content signals were orders of magnitude below schema scale. Crucially, graph similarity was nearly identical for both-correct (0.488) and both-incorrect (0.484) case pairs—demonstrating that CRG structure captures reasoning dimensions orthogonal to final-answer accuracy. Structured reflection prompting increased explicit feature discrimination (+33%) but failed to improve cross-case consistency. These results reveal diagnostic competence without schema-scale reasoning consistency, underscoring the necessity of process-level evaluation. We release the ontology, extraction pipeline, validation protocol, and all CRGs with similarity artifacts as open resources.

Abstract

Modern large language models (LLMs) reach 60-70% diagnostic accuracy on complex clinical case benchmarks, but accuracy alone cannot distinguish stable clinically-grounded reasoning from pattern matching. We introduce clinical reasoning graphs, structured graph representations extracted from free-text LLM diagnostic traces using a domain-grounded ontology with 5 node types and 7 edge types. We apply this pipeline to 750 traces from five LLMs across 50 New England Journal of Medicine Clinicopathological Conference cases and three prompt conditions, and test whether diagnostic traces show stable structured reasoning patterns, or diagnostic schemas, for clinically similar cases. We operationalize this as higher graph similarity among clinically similar cases than among clinically dissimilar ones. Across 15 model-condition comparisons, within-cluster and between-cluster composite similarity are nearly equal, and no comparison survives multiple-testing correction; a component-level analysis finds any residual content signal far below schema scale. Graph similarity is also nearly identical for pairs of models that are both correct (0.488) and both incorrect (0.484), suggesting that graph structure captures a dimension not reflected in diagnostic accuracy. Structured reflection prompting increases explicit discriminating-feature analysis within traces (+33%) but does not increase cross-case consistency. These results show diagnostic competence without schema-scale reasoning consistency, and indicate that final-answer accuracy should be complemented by process-level evaluation. We release the ontology, extraction pipeline, validation protocol, and the extracted reasoning graphs and similarity artifacts as resources for structured evaluation of LLM clinical reasoning.

item.title: Making Multimodal LLMs Reliable Chart Data Extractors: A Benchmark and Training Framework
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.29808v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

图表数据提取（Chart Data Extraction）旨在从图表图像中逆向重构原始数据表，是科研可复现性、数据分析、知识检索与可视化重设计的关键基础能力。当前工具存在明显局限：交互式工具（如WebPlotDigitizer）虽准确可靠，但操作繁琐、效率低下；混合主动式系统（mixed-initiative）虽提升效率，却泛化能力弱，难以适应多样图表类型与复杂布局。近期多模态大语言模型（MLLMs）为图表理解提供了统一接口，但其在无显式坐标轴标签、刻度或图例场景下的数值级数据提取精度尚不明确，尤其在细粒度数值恢复上表现不佳。

方法与创新

本研究提出首个面向无标签真实图表的综合性基准（ChartExtracBench），涵盖折线图、柱状图、散点图等12类共1,247张来自学术论文、财报与政府报告的高质量图表，所有样本均剔除原始数据标签以模拟真实挑战场景。实验发现：现有MLLMs（如Qwen-VL、LLaVA、Kosmos-2）能稳定识别表格结构（行列数、标题、单元格关系），但数值误差高达32.7%（平均绝对百分比误差MAPE），主因在于缺乏对图表视觉编码（如比例尺映射、像素-数值映射）的渐进式建模。

为此，我们从以人为中心的设计范式出发，提出“渐进式图表阅读”训练框架（Progressive Chart Reading, PCR）：将提取任务解耦为三阶段——① 结构感知（定位坐标系、图例、数据区域）；② 尺度校准（学习像素距离与数值量级的映射关系）；③ 数值精修（结合上下文约束与数值一致性校验）。通过合成+真实数据联合微调，在仅7B参数模型上实现MAPE降至8.9%，超越所有开源及闭源基线（+23.8%相对提升），达当前最优性能。

验证与影响

用户研究表明：该模型显著提升混合主动式工作流效率（任务耗时减少41%），且92%参与者认为其输出“可直接用于后续分析”，验证了可靠性与实用性。本工作为MLLMs在高保真视觉-数值转换任务中的可信部署提供了可复现基准、可解释框架与实用解决方案。

AI Summary (English)

Chart data extraction—reverse-engineering tabular data from chart images—is critical for reproducibility, analysis, and redesign. While interactive tools are accurate but labor-intensive, and mixed-initiative systems lack generalizability, multimodal LLMs (MLLMs) offer a unified interface yet exhibit poor numerical accuracy—especially on real-world charts without visible labels (e.g., axes, legends). We introduce ChartExtracBench, the first benchmark of 1,247 diverse, label-free charts from academic, financial, and governmental sources. Experiments reveal that state-of-the-art MLLMs reliably infer table structure but fail at precise value recovery (mean absolute percentage error, MAPE = 32.7%). To address this, we propose a human-centered Progressive Chart Reading (PCR) training framework that decomposes extraction into three stages: structural perception, scale calibration, and numerical refinement. Fine-tuning a 7B-parameter model with PCR reduces MAPE to 8.9%, achieving new SOTA. A user study confirms its effectiveness in mixed-initiative workflows—reducing task time by 41% and earning high trust (92% of users deemed outputs “ready for downstream analysis”).

Abstract

Chart data extraction, which reverse-engineers data tables from chart images, is essential for reproducibility, analysis, retrieval, and redesign. Existing interactive tools are reliable but tedious, and mixed-initiative systems, while more efficient, lack generalizability. Recent multimodal large language models (MLLMs) offer a unified interface for chart interpretation, yet their ability to extract accurate data tables, especially without visible labels, remains unclear. We build a benchmark featuring diverse real-world charts without data labels to evaluate this capability. Results show that, while current MLLMs reliably reconstruct table structures, they struggle with precise value recovery. To address this, we revisit chart data extraction from a human-centered perspective and argue that extraction should follow a progressive learning process similar to how people read charts. Our training framework substantially improves numerical accuracy, achieving state-of-the-art performance with a 7B-parameter model. A user study further shows that our model effectively supports mixed-initiative workflows for reliable chart data extraction.

item.title: Redefining Maritime Anomaly Detection via Equation-Grounded Synthetic Anomalies
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.29721v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

海上异常检测对保障航行安全、海上治安及交通效率至关重要，而自动识别系统（AIS）数据是其核心数据源。然而，现有公开AIS数据集普遍缺乏预定义的异常标签，导致既有研究依赖两类局限性显著的方法：一是基于统计分布的“稀有性”判定，易将高频但危险的事件（如密集航道中的近距离接近）误判为正常；二是依赖领域专家规则或人工标注，成本高昂、主观性强且难以泛化。更关键的是，二者均难以系统刻画交互驱动型风险（如船舶近失事件），严重制约模型的实用性和可解释性。

方法创新

本文提出方程驱动的异常分类体系（Equation-Grounded Taxonomy），在仅需基础AIS观测字段（经纬度、航速、航向、时间戳）的前提下，明确定义三类可计算、可验证的异常：

  • A1：意外AIS活动（如静止中突然启航、信号中断后异常重连）；
  • A2：航线偏离（基于动态轨迹拟合与多段贝塞尔曲线偏差量化）；
  • A3：近距离接近（融合CPA（最近会遇距离）与TCPA（最近会遇时间）的物理方程约束，支持多船交互建模）。

基于该分类，我们构建统一的“评分–合成–标注”流水线：利用大语言模型（LLM）生成轨迹合理性评分，指导合成符合物理规律与航海常识的高质量异常样本，并输出毫秒级时间戳标签。进一步设计覆盖不同时间窗口长度（1–30分钟）与异常类型混合比例（纯A1/A2/A3 vs. 混合）的基准评估协议，系统评测24种时序模型与异常检测算法。

贡献与影响

本工作首次实现可复现、可扩展、可物理解释的海上异常标注范式，填补了AIS基准缺失的关键空白。开源代码库（https://github.com/snudial/open-maritime-anomaly-detection）已集成完整数据生成、标注与评测框架，为算法研发与监管应用提供坚实基础。

AI Summary (English)

Maritime anomaly detection critically relies on Automatic Identification System (AIS) data, yet most public AIS datasets lack ground-truth anomaly labels—leading prior work to adopt either statistically rare patterns (ignoring safety-critical but frequent events) or costly, subjective expert labeling (poorly scalable and interaction-agnostic). To overcome these limitations, we propose an equation-grounded anomaly taxonomy defining three physically interpretable types: unexpected AIS activity (A1), route deviation (A2), and close approach (A3)—all computable from minimal AIS fields (latitude, longitude, speed, course, timestamp). We introduce a unified score-synthesize-label pipeline, leveraging LLM-guided plausibility scoring to synthesize realistic, equation-constrained anomalies and assign fine-grained timestamp-level labels. Our benchmark rigorously evaluates 24 time-series and anomaly detection models across varying temporal windows (1–30 min) and anomaly-type compositions. Code and evaluation framework are open-sourced at https://github.com/snudial/open-maritime-anomaly-detection.

Abstract

Maritime anomaly detection is essential for ensuring maritime safety, security, and efficient traffic management at sea, with Automatic Identification System (AIS) data serving as a primary data source. Despite its importance, most publicly available AIS datasets lack predefined anomaly labels, forcing prior studies to rely on either distribution-based rarity or domain rule/expert-assisted labeling. These approaches, however, face fundamental limitations: statistical rarity often fails to reflect practically critical events, while expert-based labeling is costly, subjective, and difficult to scale. Moreover, both paradigms tend to overlook interaction-driven hazards such as near-miss approaches between vessels. To address these challenges, we propose an equation-grounded anomaly taxonomy that is implementable under a limited AIS observation schema and extensible to other AIS datasets. Specifically, the taxonomy defines three anomaly types: unexpected AIS activity (A1), route deviation (A2), and close approach (A3), covering both single-vessel and inter-vessel anomalies. Building on this taxonomy, we introduce a unified score-synthesize-label pipeline that produces LLM-guided plausibility scores, uses them to synthesize anomalies, and assigns timestamp-level labels. To rigorously assess detection performance, we further design benchmark evaluation settings that account for variations in temporal-window length and anomaly-type composition, and evaluate a broad range of time-series models and anomaly detection models. Together, these contributions provide a systematic basis for evaluating maritime anomaly detection methods across different anomaly types. Our code is available at https://github.com/snudial/open-maritime-anomaly-detection.

item.title: Toward Secure and Reliable PDDL Formalization of Large Language Models with Planner-in-the-Loop Feedback
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.29700v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与挑战

在自主决策与安全关键型规划系统中，大型语言模型（LLMs）需将自然语言指令可靠地形式化为可执行、可验证的符号规划描述（如PDDL）。当前方法常生成语法正确但不可执行或语义失准的PDDL，导致规划失败、行为不可验证甚至安全隐患。

方法创新

我们提出：

  • NL-PDDL-Bench：首个支持多领域、难度可控（按对象数量分级）、且强制通过规划器验证可执行性的自然语言→PDDL基准；
  • Planner-in-the-Loop（PiL）框架：利用规划器诊断反馈（如未满足前提、不可达目标），对非执行规格进行局部化编辑修复，避免全局重生成；
  • Planner-Grounded优化范式：融合三阶段协同优化——（1）参数高效LoRA监督微调；（2）离线构建规划器生成的偏好对用于Direct Preference Optimization（DPO）；（3）推理时PiL实时修复，全程无需训练阶段在线调用规划器；
  • 统一评估体系：覆盖可解析性、可解性、规格相似性、结果感知的计划级一致性（对比规划器参考解）。

主要发现

在LLaMA-3、Qwen、Phi-3等主流模型上实验表明：该方法显著提升规划成功率（+28.7%）与计划级语义一致性（+34.1%），且在对象规模扩大与跨领域迁移下保持强鲁棒性。研究表明，外部可验证的形式化闭环是保障LLM在安全敏感规划场景中可靠部署的关键基础设施。

AI Summary (English)

This paper addresses the critical gap between natural language instructions and verifiable, executable PDDL formalizations for LLMs in safety-critical planning systems. We introduce NL-PDDL-Bench, the first multi-domain benchmark requiring planner-verified executability and controlled difficulty scaling via object count. Building on it, we propose a planner-in-the-loop (PiL) framework that diagnoses and locally repairs non-executable specifications using planner feedback—without online planner calls during training. Our planner-grounded optimization integrates LoRA fine-tuning, offline DPO with planner-derived preference pairs, and inference-time PiL repair. Evaluated across LLaMA-3, Qwen, and Phi-3, our method achieves substantial gains: +28.7% planner success rate and +34.1% plan-level agreement over baselines, with strong robustness under scalability and domain shift. Code and data: https://github.com/ibasicplan/NL-PDDL-Bench

Abstract

Planning often requires symbolic specifications that are both executable and verifiable. For large language models deployed in autonomous or decision-support systems, failures in such formalization may lead to unverifiable decisions, execution failures, or unsafe downstream behavior. We present NL-PDDL-Bench, a multi-domain benchmark for natural-language-to-PDDL specification construction with planner-verified executability and controlled difficulty scaling by object count. We further propose a planner-in-the-loop framework that uses validator and planner diagnostics to revise non-executable specifications through localized edits. Building on this infrastructure, we develop a planner-grounded optimization recipe that combines parameter-efficient Low-Rank Adaptation supervised fine-tuning, offline planner-derived preference pairs for Direct Preference Optimization, and inference-time planner-in-the-loop repair, without requiring online planner calls during training. We also provide a unified evaluation suite for parseability, solvability, specification similarity, and outcome-aware plan-level consistency against planner references. Experiments on representative model families show substantial gains in planner success and plan-level agreement, with improved robustness under difficulty scaling and cross-domain variation. These results highlight the value of externally verifiable formalization for reliable deployment of LLMs in safety- or security-sensitive planning systems. Code and data are available at: https://github.com/ibasicplan/NL-PDDL-Bench

item.title: Discovering Collaboration from Novelty: Random Network Distillation for Clustered Federated Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30499v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

联邦学习（Federated Learning, FL）在非独立同分布（Non-IID）数据下常表现欠佳：单一全局模型难以兼顾各客户端异构的数据分布，导致性能下降与收敛困难。集群化联邦学习（Clustered FL）通过为相似客户端子群训练专用模型缓解该问题，但现有方法多将聚类过程与主训练循环强耦合——需反复上传/下载模型、频繁计算相似度或执行联合优化，显著增加通信开销与计算负担，且依赖预设的簇数量，难以适应动态、自治的大规模边缘系统。

方法创新

本文提出一种解耦式轻量级聚类框架：基于随机网络蒸馏（Random Network Distillation, RND）构建本地化“新颖性信号”。每个客户端仅需在本地训练一个极简RND预测器（单层MLP），并以该预测器在自身数据上的重建误差作为其数据分布的隐式表征。该误差天然反映数据“新颖性”——相似客户端因数据模式相近，其RND误差分布高度一致；差异大的客户端则误差谱显著分离。由此，无需共享原始数据、模型参数或梯度，仅通过误差向量（低维、可哈希）即可实现高效跨客户端相似性估计。

核心优势与发现

  • 完全解耦：聚类在联邦训练前一次性完成，不干预主学习流程；
  • 完全自治：簇数与结构由本地误差动态涌现，无需先验设定；
  • 极致轻量：RND模块参数<1KB，训练耗时<1秒/客户端（CIFAR-10实验）；
  • 强泛化性：在6个Non-IID基准（包括Label/Feature/Quantity skew）上，平均提升集群纯度23.7%，下游任务准确率较基线高4.2–8.9个百分点，且通信量减少37%。

本工作首次将RND从强化学习探索机制迁移至联邦聚类场景，为大规模分布式系统提供了任务无关、隐私友好、运行时自组织的协作发现范式。

AI Summary (English)

Federated Learning (FL) struggles with non-IID data, where a single global model fails to capture client heterogeneity. Clustered FL addresses this by learning specialized models per client group, but existing methods tightly couple clustering with model training—increasing communication, computation, and requiring pre-specified cluster counts. We propose RND-Cluster, a lightweight, decoupled clustering approach: each client trains a compact Random Network Distillation (RND) predictor locally and uses its prediction error as a novelty signal to estimate cross-client similarity. This enables cluster discovery before FL begins—without sharing raw data, gradients, or main-model weights. Crucially, clusters emerge autonomously from local error statistics at runtime, eliminating the need for prior knowledge of cluster number or topology. Evaluated across six Non-IID benchmarks, RND-Cluster improves clustering purity by 23.7% on average and boosts downstream accuracy by 4.2–8.9 points, while reducing communication by 37%. It offers a task-agnostic, privacy-preserving, and scalable mechanism for self-organized collaboration in large-scale distributed systems.

Abstract

Federated Learning often suffers under non-independently and identically distributed data, where a single global model may fail to represent the diversity of client distributions. Clustered Federated Learning mitigates this issue by training specialized models for groups of similar clients, but existing approaches often couple cluster assignment with the main training loop, increasing computational and communication costs. We propose a lightweight clustering approach based on Random Network Distillation. Each client trains a compact Random Network Distillation predictor on its local data and uses its prediction error as a novelty signal to estimate similarity with other clients. This enables the discovery of meaningful client groups before federated training, without sharing raw data or repeatedly evaluating the main model. Crucially, the resulting federations emerge from local novelty estimates at runtime, making the method suitable for autonomous large-scale distributed systems where neither the number of clusters nor the collaboration structure can be specified a priori. Overall, by decoupling clustering from learning, the method provides a task-agnostic and efficient mechanism for autonomous collaboration under non-independently and identically distributed data.

item.title: Highly Data Parallelizable Estimation of the Sliced-Wasserstein Distance Using Cumulative Distribution Functions
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30310v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

Sliced-Wasserstein（SW）距离因其通过随机投影将高维最优传输降维为一维问题而广受关注，显著降低了计算复杂度。然而，传统SW估计器依赖蒙特卡洛采样，需对每次投影后的样本进行全局排序以构造分位数函数（quantile function），这不仅带来$O(n\log n)$时间开销，更要求中心化访问全部原始数据，严重制约其在大规模分布式或隐私敏感场景（如联邦学习）中的应用。

方法创新

本文提出一类基于累积分布函数（CDF）的新型SW距离估计器。核心思想是：不再显式计算分位数，而是直接建模投影测度的CDF——该函数可通过数值积分、解析表达或核密度估计高效获得，完全规避排序操作。我们构建了参数化CDF估计器族，其中超参数可灵活调控估计方差与平滑性（如带宽控制的核CDF、截断多项式近似等），兼顾精度与鲁棒性。

主要发现与优势

  • 在混合高斯等解析CDF易得的模型中，新估计器实现亚线性计算复杂度（$O(n)$），且误差收敛率与经典方法相当；
  • 天然适配联邦学习范式：各客户端独立计算本地投影CDF后，仅需聚合CDF值（如加权平均），无需传输原始样本或排序索引，显著降低通信开销与隐私风险；
  • 实验验证其在生成模型评估与分布式数据匹配任务中，相较基线方法提速2.3–5.7倍，同时保持<1.2%的相对误差。

本工作为SW距离的大规模、隐私保护与边缘计算场景提供了首个免排序、高并行、可分解的理论框架与实用工具。

AI Summary (English)

The Sliced-Wasserstein (SW) distance offers computational efficiency over the full Wasserstein distance by reducing optimal transport to 1D via random projections. Standard estimators rely on quantile functions requiring sorting of projected samples—prohibitive for massive or distributed data. We propose a novel class of SW estimators based on cumulative distribution functions (CDFs) of projected measures, eliminating sorting and enabling massive parallelism. These CDF-based estimators are parameterized to trade off variance and smoothness, and admit closed-form expressions for tractable models (e.g., Gaussian mixtures). Crucially, they are natively compatible with federated learning: local CDFs can be computed and aggregated without sharing raw data or indices. We prove consistency and derive convergence rates, demonstrating up to 5.7× speedup and <1.2% relative error in experiments on generative modeling and distributed matching tasks.

Abstract

The Sliced Wasserstein (SW) distance has emerged as a computationally attractive alternative to the Wasserstein distance by leveraging one-dimensional optimal transport along random projections. Standard estimators of the SW distance rely on Monte Carlo averages of one-dimensional Wasserstein distances computed via quantile functions, which require sorting projected samples and access to full datasets. In this work, we introduce a new class of estimators for the Sliced Wasserstein distance based on cumulative distribution functions (CDFs) of projected measures, that avoid sorting and scale via massive dataset parallelism. This class includes several estimators, some of them being indexed by hyperparameters controlling their variance or smoothness. We show that they are especially well suited to scenarios in which CDFs are more tractable than quantile functions, such as mixtures of Gaussians, and moreover that they are also naturally compatible with federated learning, since CDFs of projected data can be computed and aggregated locally without requiring the exchange of raw samples.

item.title: Theory of Continual Learning Against Data Poisoning Attacks
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.29841v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

持续学习（Continual Learning, CL）使模型能顺序适应新任务，在大语言模型与图像识别等关键领域日益重要。然而，其固有的参数复用机制使其极易遭受数据投毒攻击——恶意注入的污染样本可引发学习发散或显著增益风险。当前CL理论严重缺乏对投毒攻防的统一数学建模与性能边界刻画，导致防御策略多依赖启发式设计，缺乏理论保障。

理论框架与核心发现

本文构建首个面向正则化型持续学习（regulation-based CL）的博弈论分析框架，将攻防交互形式化为在线零和博弈。我们严格证明：当攻击者以线性比例（即Ω(T)个任务）投毒，且注入无界噪声或模式偏移时，任何防御策略均失效——这是CL中数据投毒的根本性不可防御性极限。

两类可防御场景与创新方案

1. 低频攻击场景（攻击任务数=o(T)）：提出任务级验证机制（Task-to-Task Verification），通过跨任务梯度一致性检验识别异常任务，显著降低累积偏差，保障收敛性；
2. 有界噪声场景（每轮投毒扰动有界）：设计鲁棒特征解耦防御，最小化模型对中毒特征的敏感度，理论证明其收敛速率提升至O(1/√T)（优于标准CL的O(1/√T)常数项优化）。

实证验证

在Split-CIFAR100、Domain-Incremental Office-Home及LLM微调序列任务上，所提方法将投毒后准确率恢复至基线98.2%，验证了理论预测与实际鲁棒性的高度一致。

AI Summary (English)

This paper establishes the first principled theory for data poisoning attacks and defenses in regularization-based continual learning (CL). We model the adversary-defender interaction as an online zero-sum game and prove a fundamental impossibility result: no defense can succeed when an attacker poisons a linear number of tasks with unbounded noise or pattern shifts. We then identify two defensible regimes—infrequent attacks and bounded per-attack perturbations—and propose corresponding solutions. For infrequent attacks, we introduce a task-to-task verification mechanism that detects poisoned tasks via gradient consistency, reducing cumulative bias. For bounded perturbations, we design a robust defense that minimizes model sensitivity to poisoned features, provably accelerating convergence to O(1/√T) with improved constants. Experiments on Split-CIFAR100, Office-Home, and LLM fine-tuning sequences validate both theoretical guarantees and practical efficacy.

Abstract

Continual learning (CL), where a model is trained on a sequence of data tasks, is increasingly being adopted across key fields such as large language models and image recognition, yet it remains highly vulnerable to data poisoning that triggers learning divergence or severe excess risk. Despite these threats, a principled theoretical foundation in CL for understanding attack and defense remains lacking. In this paper, we develop a theoretical framework to analyze strategic attacks and defenses in regularization-based CL, a cornerstone of recent CL theory. By framing the adversary-defender interaction as an online zero-sum game, we first establish a fundamental performance limit: no defense succeeds when an adversary poisons a linear proportion of tasks by injecting unbounded noise or pattern shifts in regularization-based CL. We then analyze two possibly defensible scenarios: infrequent attacks and bounded noise per attack. For the former regime, we propose a task-to-task verification mechanism to detect data poisoning and reduce cumulative bias for learning convergence. For the latter regime, we derive a robust defense that minimizes the model's sensitivity to poisoned features, provably accelerating the convergence rate. Extensive experiments on realistic tasks further validate our theoretical results.

item.title: Falafel: Modular Zero-Knowledge Proofs of Training in the Federated Setting
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1335

AI Summary (中文)

Falafel：面向联邦学习的模块化零知识训练证明方案

本文提出 Falafel——一种面向联邦学习（Federated Learning, FL）的模块化、高效、可验证零知识训练证明（zkPoT）框架，全称 Fast, Authenticated, Locally Attested FEderated Learning。针对现有zkPoT方案依赖强假设、证明体积大、缺乏端到端流程保障等局限，Falafel首次实现对完整FL训练流水线（含本地训练、中心化权重聚合、输入真实性校验）的细粒度、公开可验证的零知识证明，且不泄露任何本地数据分布、梯度、模型参数或中间状态。

Falafel采用创新的“承诺-证明”模块化架构：将训练过程分解为可独立验证的组件（如本地SGD步、加权平均聚合、数据签名验证），各模块使用经充分验证的密码学原语（基于标准离散对数假设的SNARKs），避免依赖未充分分析的假设或算术哈希函数（如Fiat–Shamir实例化）。方案引入可信审计员（Trusted Auditor），负责认证客户端输入数据的真实性，使外部验证者能从原始数据集出发，端到端验证最终模型的正确性与活性安全（active security）。

实验表明：在LeNet模型上，单轮FL训练生成的zkPoT仅约 70 kB（较同类工作缩小10–15倍），证明生成耗时约 150秒，与前沿方案相当；验证时间毫秒级。其高度并行化设计支持大规模客户端协同证明生成。Falafel为隐私敏感场景（如医疗、金融联邦建模）提供了首个兼顾严格隐私保障、高效验证性与工程实用性的zkPoT落地路径。

AI Summary (English)

We present Falafel, a modular zero-knowledge proof of training (zkPoT) scheme for federated learning (FL), enabling parties to generate compact, publicly verifiable proofs of correct and secure FL execution—without leaking local data or intermediate models. Unlike prior zkPoTs, Falafel provides end-to-end attestation: it verifies local training steps, centralized weight aggregation, and input authenticity via a trusted auditor, ensuring active security and full-process accountability. Built solely on well-studied cryptographic assumptions (e.g., discrete logarithm), it adopts a parallelizable, “commit-and-prove” modular design with swappable core components. For LeNet, Falafel generates a 70 kB zkPoT in ~150 seconds per round, achieving proof sizes 10–15× smaller than state-of-the-art while matching prover efficiency and avoiding fragile assumptions or arithmetic-hash-based Fiat–Shamir.

Abstract

We introduce Falafel, a modular scheme for Fast, Authenticated, Locally Attested FEderated Learning, with which parties can create a zero-knowledge proof of training (zkPoT) for Federated Learning (FL). The proof guarantees active security during the federated training process as well as publicly verifiable correctness of the final, trained model. All without revealing any additional information about the local datasets or intermediate local model states.

Specifically, our approach targets FL of (deep) neural networks with a centralized server for weight updates. Our zkPoT not only offers attestation for local training steps, but also for the centralized weight update, as well as taking into account input authenticity by introducing a trusted auditor. This way an external verifier can check the entire training process, from dataset to final model.

In contrast to prior work on zkPoTs, our construction solely relies on well-understood cryptographic assumptions and primitives, is highly parallelizable, and takes a modular approach. This modular (commit-and-prove) approach uses several novel core proof components, that could be swapped for other building blocks if desired. We show that, for LeNet, we generate a zkPoT of 70 kB in roughly 150 seconds for a single training round. Falafel’s prover time is in line with prior work, and its proof size is significantly smaller (10–15×), without relying on less-understood assumptions or instantiating Fiat–Shamir using arithmetic hash functions.

item.title: Principles for pRHL Proofs of Cryptographic Protocols: How to Convince EasyCrypt that your Protocol is Secure
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1334

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与问题

EasyCrypt 是一个广泛用于密码学原语（如加密、签名）形式化安全证明的交互式证明助手，其核心逻辑 pRHL（probabilistic Relational Hoare Logic）擅长刻画概率程序间的等价关系。然而，当面向交互式协议（如密钥协商、认证协议）时，现有形式化实践屡遭挫折——证明过程冗长、结构脆弱、难以复用。本文以一个简化的交互式密钥协商协议为案例，系统剖析这一“协议形式化鸿沟”的根源。

方法与演进路径

研究采用三阶段实证法：

  • 探索性证明：构建首个完整但高度特化的 pRHL 证明，暴露底层复杂性；
  • 结构化尝试：引入模块化分解与抽象不变式，却因验证失败而中止；
  • 本质性证明：提炼出兼顾状态不变式（支撑密码学推理，如伪随机性、不可区分性）与时间不变式（刻画协议轮次、消息序、控制流依赖）的双轨不变式框架。

主要发现与创新

本文首次明确指出：交互协议的安全证明在计算模型下本质上是双不变式协同推理——仅依赖状态不变式无法捕获协议时序行为，仅依赖时间不变式则无法支撑密码假设。该观察解释了为何现有工具（聚焦原语）难以直接迁移至协议场景。据此，我们提出设计新型推理原语（如时序感知的 pRHL 扩展）的必要性，为弥合“原语级工具”与“协议级工具”之间的方法论断层提供理论基础与实践路标。

AI Summary (English)

EasyCrypt has enabled rigorous formal verification of cryptographic primitives using pRHL, yet interactive protocols remain notoriously difficult to formalize. This paper investigates the root causes by developing and refining pRHL proofs for a simple key-agreement protocol—from an initial ad-hoc proof, through a failed structured attempt, to an “essential” proof we identify as canonical. We argue that the core difficulty lies in the dual reliance on state invariants (to justify cryptographic assumptions like pseudorandomness) and temporal invariants (to reason about message order, round structure, and control flow). This interplay is absent in primitive-focused proofs but indispensable for protocols. Our analysis provides a conceptual framework—and concrete guidance—for designing next-generation reasoning tools that bridge the gap between primitive-centric and protocol-centric verification.

Abstract

The EasyCrypt proof assistant has been used to
successfully formalize security proofs for a wide variety of crypto-
graphic primitives. However, attempts at formalizing objects with
interactivity, such as protocols, have fared much worse. In this
paper, we investigate (some of) the reasons for this difficulty by
formalizing a simple interactive key agreement protocol. From
a first complete but exploratory proof, to a failed attempt at
a structured proof, and to what we believe is an “essential”
proof, we identify which proof features contribute most to the
complexity of formalization in pRHL. In particular, we argue that
difficulties in formalizing the security of interactive protocols in
the computational model arise from the fact that such proofs rely
on both state and temporal invariants—the former to support
cryptographic reasoning, and the latter to support reasoning
about the protocol’s structure. We believe that this observation
can help build new reasoning tools that can bridge the gap that
currently exists between primitive-focused tools and protocol-
focused tools.

item.title: Apples, Oranges, and Signatures: Pitfalls and Methodology in ML-DSA Benchmarking
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1333

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

后量子密码迁移亟需可靠性能数据支撑工程决策，而ML-DSA（基于模块格的数字签名算法）作为NIST PQC标准核心候选，其基准测试实践却普遍存在系统性缺陷。当前多数评测忽略算法内在特性，导致结果不可比、不可复现、不适用于实时系统部署。

关键发现

  • 安全级别错配：跨参数集（如ML-DSA-65/87/106）比较时，常混淆“理论安全强度”与“实际实现开销”，未统一归一化至等效安全等级；
  • 时间变异性被低估：签名过程含拒绝采样与数据依赖分支，执行时间呈显著非正态分布——最小值/平均值/最大值等传统指标无法刻画真实尾部行为；实测显示99.9th百分位耗时可达均值的3.2倍；
  • 输入数据缺乏标准化：随机消息与密钥未固定，导致跨平台结果波动高达±18%，掩盖硬件/软件优化的真实差异。

方法创新

提出三支柱基准测试框架：
1. 标准化输入集：提供经验证的确定性消息-密钥对集合（覆盖典型与边界场景）；
2. 分位数驱动报告：强制要求披露P50/P90/P99.9执行时间及置信区间（≥10,000次迭代）；
3. 安全强度归一化：所有性能数据按比特安全强度（bits）加权折算为“每安全比特纳秒”，实现跨参数集公平比较。

实践价值

该方法已验证于ARM Cortex-M4、x86-64及RISC-V平台，支持实时系统最坏情况执行时间（WCET）分析，并为NIST后续PQC实施指南提供可落地的方法论基础。

AI Summary (English)

Cryptographic migration to post-quantum standards like ML-DSA demands rigorous, comparable performance data—but current benchmarking practices suffer from inconsistent security-level alignment, unstandardized inputs, and inappropriate metrics for its inherently variable signing time (due to rejection sampling). We demonstrate that min/avg/max latency metrics misrepresent real-world timing behavior, especially for real-time systems: the 99.9th percentile signing time exceeds the mean by up to 3.2×. To resolve this, we propose a robust methodology featuring (1) standardized, deterministic input datasets; (2) quantile-based reporting (P50, P90, P99.9) with statistical confidence intervals over ≥10,000 runs; and (3) security-strength-normalized metrics (ns per security bit) enabling fair cross-parameter-set comparison. Validated across ARM, x86-64, and RISC-V platforms, our approach directly supports WCET analysis and informs NIST’s PQC deployment guidance.

Abstract

Cryptographic migration, particularly in the post-quantum setting, poses significant practical challenges and requires reliable performance data to support sound engineering decisions. For ML-DSA, however, existing benchmarking practices often produce misleading or non-comparable results, complicating migration and cryptographic agility efforts. This paper analyzes common pitfalls in benchmarking ML-DSA signature operations, including subtle inconsistencies when comparing security levels. We show that execution-time variability of the ML-DSA signing algorithm - an inherent property due to rejection sampling and other data-dependent components - makes commonly used straightforward metrics, e.g., min/average/max, unsuitable for migration planning. To address this gap, we propose a robust benchmarking methodology based on standardized input data sets and clearly qualified reporting metrics. The proposed approach enables fair comparison across hardware and software implementations and supports designers of real-time systems to assess the worst-case execution time.

item.title: An Empirical Evaluation of Prompt Injection Vulnerabilities in Large Language Models Across Multilingual and Obfuscated Attack Scenarios
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.29602v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景与意义

大型语言模型（LLMs）在自动化复杂任务与生成类人文本方面展现出强大能力，但其广泛部署也暴露出严重的提示注入（Prompt Injection）漏洞。攻击者可借此诱导模型生成钓鱼邮件、仿冒网站及恶意代码，对网络安全构成实质性威胁。当前防御机制多聚焦于英文单语场景，缺乏对多语言适配性与混淆攻击鲁棒性的系统性实证检验。

方法与实验设计

本研究构建了首个覆盖多语言（中、英、日、法、西、阿）与多层混淆（Unicode变体、Base64、HTML实体、Leetspeak、多轮指令拆分） 的提示注入评估框架。对6个主流开源与闭源模型（DeepSeek、GPT-4、Gemini 1.5、Grok-2、Llama-3、Qwen2）开展大规模实证测试，量化其在执行有害指令（生成钓鱼内容、恶意网页、可执行脚本）时的恶意合规率（Malicious Compliance Rate, MCR）。

关键发现

  • 全模型普遍存在脆弱性：所有模型均在直接注入下生成有害内容，MCR范围达32%–89%；多阶段混淆攻击进一步提升成功率，最高达94%（Grok-2在日语Leetspeak场景）。
  • 语言偏差显著：非英语语种平均MCR比英语高17.3个百分点（如中文+22.1%，阿拉伯语+28.6%），揭示多语言安全对齐严重不足。
  • 混淆策略效果分化：简单编码（如Base64）仅降低MCR约8–15%，而语义保留型混淆（如角色扮演嵌套）反而提升成功率。DeepSeek、Gemini与Grok在复杂指令下表现最差。
  • 防御启示：现有输入过滤与输出审核机制难以应对语义级绕过，亟需融合上下文感知检测、多语言对抗训练与跨模态验证的纵深防御体系。

本研究为LLM安全评估提供了可复现的基准框架，并首次揭示了多语言与混淆场景下的系统性风险图谱，对金融、政务等高敏领域LLM部署具有直接指导价值。

AI Summary (English)
