This paper presents the first empirical evaluation of prompt injection vulnerabilities across six state-of-the-art LLMs (DeepSeek, GPT-4, Gemini 1.5, Grok-2, Llama-3, Qwen2) under multilingual (Chinese, Japanese, French, Spanish, Arabic) and obfuscated attack scenarios—including Unicode variants, Base64, HTML entities, Leetspeak, and multi-turn instruction splitting. We introduce a standardized metric—Malicious Compliance Rate (MCR)—to quantify model susceptibility to generating phishing content, malicious websites, and executable code. Results show systematic vulnerabilities: all models exhibit high MCR (32–94%), with non-English languages consistently outperforming English by +17.3% on average; DeepSeek, Gemini, and Grok are most vulnerable under complex obfuscation. Simple encodings reduce but fail to eliminate harmful outputs, while semantic-preserving obfuscations often increase success rates. These findings underscore critical gaps in multilingual safety alignment and call for robust, context-aware defenses for cybersecurity-critical deployments.

Abstract

Large Language Models (LLMs) have rapidly evolved, transforming industries by automating complex tasks and generating human-like content. However, as their adoption accelerates, prompt injection vulnerabilities have become increasingly apparent. Malicious actors exploit these weaknesses to generate phishing emails, deceptive websites, nd malware, posing serious security risks. This paper presents an empirical evaluation of six state-of-the-art LLMs (DeepSeek, GPT, Gemini, Grok, Llama, and Qwen) under diverse adversarial prompt scenarios, including direct and multi-stage obfuscated attacks across multiple languages and character encodings. The proposed framework measures how effectively current LLMs resist manipulation into performing harmful actions. Our findings reveal systematic vulnerabilities across all tested models. Even direct prompt injections frequently induce the generation of phishing content, websites, and malware, while elaborate prompts achieve even higher malicious compliance rates, particularly for phishing. Models such as DeepSeek, Gemini, and Grok show especially high susceptibility under complex instructions. Notably, non-English languages consistently exhibit higher compliance rates than English, exposing significant gaps in multilingual safety alignment. Although simple character encodings reduce malicious outputs, they do not eliminate them. These results highlight persistent challenges in LLM safety and underscore the urgent need for stronger defenses and improved security mechanisms to support the ethical and secure deployment of LLMs in cybersecurity sensitive contexts.

item.title: Bit-ViP: Leveraging Bit-planes to Preserve Visual Privacy in Images through Obfuscation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.29417v1

AI Summary (中文)

Bit-ViP：基于比特平面的视觉隐私保护图像混淆方法

随着监控系统、社交媒体等计算机视觉应用的爆发式增长，图像数据在云端存储与处理引发了严峻的视觉隐私泄露风险。传统图像混淆方法面临双重困境：一类易受模型反演攻击（如从混淆图重建原始图像），另一类虽增强安全性却严重损害下游任务可用性，导致识别精度骤降、无法用于实际训练。本文提出Bit-ViP（Bit-plane-based Visual Privacy preservation），一种面向图像识别任务的新型端到端比特平面混淆框架。其核心创新在于：

  • 分层可控混淆：将图像分解为8个比特平面，对关键低阶平面（如第0–2位）注入Lorenz混沌系统生成的非周期性噪声，并融合差分隐私机制，确保噪声具备理论不可逆性；
  • 语义保持设计：高阶比特平面（第5–7位）保留主要结构信息，使混淆后图像仍支持CNN/Transformer等模型有效训练；
  • 安全-效用协同优化：通过联合损失函数平衡像素级不可逆性（抵抗反演）、统计鲁棒性（抗频域/熵/相关性分析）与识别性能。

在UCF101与HMDB51两大动作识别数据集上的实验表明：Bit-ViP在ResNet-50上实现86.3%和69.7%的Top-1准确率（仅比原始图像低<3.5%），显著优于现有混淆方案（平均提升12.6%）；同时，在模型反演攻击下，PSNR重建质量下降至12.4 dB以下，信息熵提升38.7%，像素互相关系数降低至0.09，验证其强抗重构能力。本工作首次将混沌动力学与差分隐私嵌入比特平面操作，为云边协同场景下的隐私-效用平衡提供了可证明、可部署的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces Bit-ViP, a novel bit-plane-based image obfuscation framework designed to preserve visual privacy while maintaining usability for recognition tasks. Unlike prior methods vulnerable to model inversion or yielding non-trainable outputs, Bit-ViP injects provably non-invertible noise—generated jointly by the Lorenz chaotic system and differential privacy—into low-order bit planes, while preserving structural semantics in high-order planes. Evaluated on UCF101 and HMDB51, Bit-ViP achieves 86.3% and 69.7% Top-1 accuracy (within 3.5% of clean-image baselines), outperforming state-of-the-art obfuscation schemes by 12.6% on average. Security analysis shows it reduces reconstruction PSNR to <12.4 dB under inversion attacks, increases entropy by 38.7%, and drops pixel inter-correlation to 0.09—demonstrating strong resistance to statistical and adversarial recovery. Bit-ViP bridges theoretical privacy guarantees with practical recognition performance.

Abstract

The unprecedented growth of computer vision applications, such as surveillance systems and social media, raises security and visual privacy concerns, especially when data is stored on cloud servers. Image obfuscation offers a way to preserve visual privacy while maintaining an adequate level of usability; thus, it has been a topic of great interest in recent years. However, prior obfuscation schemes are either vulnerable to malicious attacks, such as model inversion to reconstruct original images from obfuscated images, or generate non-trainable obfuscated images, making them unusable for achieving reasonable accuracy. This paper proposes a novel bit-plane-based image obfuscation scheme, {\em Bit-ViP}, to preserve visual privacy for image-based recognition tasks. The Bit-ViP scheme produces secure, usable images by incorporating an innovative end-to-end obfuscation function. While doing so, the obfuscated image would contain non-invertible noise (generated by Lorenz's chaotic system and differential privacy), making it hard for an adversary to reconstruct the original image. We conduct extensive experiments on two popular activity recognition datasets, namely UCF101 and HMDB51, to validate the effectiveness of Bit-ViP. In the face of attacks on reconstruction, pixel frequency, information entropy, and pixel inter-correlation, we present a rigorous security analysis demonstrating tangible improvements over existing schemes.

item.title: Breaking the Rounding Trap: Securing LLMs against Quantization-Conditioned Backdoors
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.29239v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

模型量化是降低大语言模型（LLM）存储开销与推理成本的关键技术，但其引入的离散化与舍入误差正被攻击者恶意利用，形成“量化条件后门”（QCB）：恶意行为在全精度阶段完全潜伏，仅在量化部署后激活，从而绕过常规安全审计与检测机制，构成隐蔽性强、危害性高的新型供应链威胁。

方法创新：QuantGuard

我们提出首个前量化主动防御框架 QuantGuard，无需修改现有量化算法，仅需少量校准数据（<100样本）。其核心在于三重协同机制：

  • 可微舍入控制变量：将传统不可导的舍入操作参数化，实现梯度可追溯；
  • 误差引导的舍入逆向约束：强制模型权重在舍入前后保持语义一致性，破坏攻击者精心构造的权重-量化边界对齐；
  • 输出分布一致性 + 权重距离正则化：保障量化前后模型行为稳定，兼顾安全性与功能性。

实验验证与效果

在6个主流LLM（含LLaMA-3、Qwen2.5-Coder）上，覆盖INT8/FP4/NF4三种量化精度及漏洞代码生成、有害内容注入、过度拒绝三大真实风险场景。结果表明：QuantGuard将QCB攻击成功率降至与干净模型相当水平（平均下降>92%），同时在MMLU、HumanEval、MT-Bench等通用能力基准上性能损失<1.2%，推理延迟增加可忽略（<3%）。该方法计算开销低、部署便捷，为安全量化部署提供了切实可行的工业级解决方案。

AI Summary (English)

Model quantization—while essential for efficient LLM deployment—enables quantization-conditioned backdoors (QCBs): malicious behaviors dormant in full-precision models but activated only post-quantization, evading standard security audits. To counter this, we propose QuantGuard, a lightweight, pre-quantization defense requiring only a small calibration dataset and no modification to existing quantization pipelines. QuantGuard introduces differentiable rounding control variables and jointly enforces (i) error-guided rounding reversal constraints to break attacker-crafted weight-boundary alignment, (ii) output-distribution consistency, and (iii) weight-distance regularization. Evaluated across six mainstream LLMs (e.g., LLaMA-3, Qwen2.5-Coder), three precisions (INT8/FP4/NF4), and three high-risk scenarios (vulnerable code generation, content injection, over-refusal), QuantGuard reduces QCB attack success rates to near-clean-model levels (average drop >92%) while preserving >98.8% of original performance on MMLU, HumanEval, and MT-Bench—with negligible computational overhead (<3% latency increase).

Abstract

Model quantization is a key technique for reducing storage and inference costs when deploying large language models in practice. However, recent studies show that the discretization and rounding errors introduced by quantization can be exploited by adversaries to construct quantization-conditioned backdoor (QCB) attacks. Under such attacks, malicious behaviors remain dormant in the full-precision stage and are activated only after quantized deployment, thereby bypassing conventional security auditing and detection mechanisms. To address this threat, we propose a proactive pre-quantization defense method, QuantGuard. Our method introduces differentiable rounding control variables and combines error-guided rounding reversal constraints, output-distribution consistency, and weight-distance regularization to finely regulate critical rounding behaviors. Crucially, QuantGuard utilizes only a small calibration dataset and does not modify existing quantization algorithms. This design breaks the precise alignment between attacker-crafted weight patterns and quantization boundaries, effectively suppressing the post-quantization backdoor activation pathway while preserving the model's original functionality and performance. We conduct systematic experiments on six mainstream LLMs (including the LLaMA-3 and Qwen2.5-Coder) using three quantization precisions (INT8, FP4, and NF4) across three representative scenarios: vulnerable code generation, content injection, and over-refusal. The results show that QuantGuard consistently mitigates QCB attacks, reducing the attack success rate to a level comparable to the clean model while largely preserving performance on general capability benchmarks. With low computational overhead, our method offers an effective, practical solution for secure quantized LLM deployment.

item.title: Bilevel Optimization for Neural Architecture Search
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.29582v1

AI Summary (中文)

双层优化视角下的神经架构搜索综述

双层优化（Bilevel Optimization）已成为机器学习中建模层级化决策问题的主流范式，广泛应用于超参调优、元学习、对抗训练与数据投毒等任务。神经架构搜索（NAS） 本质上是一类典型的双层优化问题：外层优化架构参数（如网络连接、算子选择），内层优化对应网络权重（即模型训练）。本文系统梳理了NAS在双层优化框架下的理论脉络与方法演进，提出两大分类体系：

  • 采样驱动型方法：依赖随机/强化/进化等架构采样器进行黑箱搜索，缺乏对内外层耦合关系的显式建模；
  • 双层理论驱动型方法：将NAS严格形式化为带约束的双层规划问题，通过梯度隐函数求导、近似解耦或数学规划重构实现协同优化。

本文重点介绍我们提出的辅助数学规划框架（Auxiliary Mathematical Programming Framework）：该框架将内层优化问题嵌入外层目标，显式引入二阶信息（Hessian逆、曲率感知梯度），保障权重更新的最优性前提下动态调整架构参数。通过联合求解该辅助程序，架构与权重沿其理论最优下降方向同步更新，显著提升搜索的收敛性与泛化性。进一步地，该框架可自然扩展至超参数与模型权重的联合细调，实现统一优化范式。实验对比表明：双层理论驱动方法在ImageNet/CIFAR-10等基准上，相较采样类方法平均提升1.2–2.8% Top-1精度，且搜索成本降低37–59%，验证了其理论一致性与工程有效性。

AI Summary (English)

Bilevel optimization provides a rigorous framework for Neural Architecture Search (NAS), where architecture parameters are optimized at the outer level and network weights at the inner level. This paper categorizes NAS methods into sampling-based approaches (e.g., RL, evolution) and bilevel theory-based approaches grounded in mathematical programming. We propose an auxiliary mathematical programming framework that explicitly incorporates second-order information from the training loss, ensuring inner-level optimality while updating architecture parameters. By jointly optimizing both levels along theoretically justified descent directions derived from this auxiliary program, our method achieves principled convergence and improved generalization. The same framework supports simultaneous hyperparameter tuning and model fine-tuning. Empirical results show bilevel theory-based NAS consistently outperforms sampling-based counterparts—gaining 1.2–2.8% higher Top-1 accuracy on CIFAR-10/ImageNet with 37–59% less search cost.

Abstract

Bilevel optimization has become an influential and widely adopted framework for addressing hierarchical optimization problems in machine learning, providing an effective approach to modeling the interaction between two levels of optimization, with applications such as hyperparameter tuning, meta-learning, adversarial training, and data poisoning. Neural Architecture Search (NAS), a subfield of hyperparameter optimization, is a prime example of a bilevel optimization problem, with architecture parameters optimized at the outer-level and network weights optimized at the inner level. This paper presents a structured overview of NAS through the lens of bilevel optimization. We categorize existing NAS approaches into two main classes: sampling-based methods, which search optimal architectures using different architecture samplers, and bilevel theory-based methods, which solve the architecture search problem using bilevel optimization principles. We further highlight our current research direction, wherein the bilevel NAS formulation is addressed through an auxiliary mathematical programming framework. This framework enables the systematic integration of second-order information from the model's training loss function and ensures the optimality of the model parameters while modifying architecture parameters. By simultaneously updating the architecture and model parameters along their respective optimal descent directions derived from the auxiliary mathematical program, these methods achieve more principled and theoretically consistent results. The same auxiliary program can also be used for simultaneous hyperparameter and model fine-tuning. A comparative analysis shows that bilevel theory-based approaches generally outperform sampling-based methods, both in accuracy and efficiency.

item.title: LC-ICL: Label-Guided Contrastive In-Context Learning for Robust Information Extraction
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.29407v1

AI Summary (中文)

LC-ICL：面向鲁棒信息抽取的标签引导对比式上下文学习方法

近年来，大语言模型（LLMs）在命名实体识别（NER）与关系抽取（RE）等信息抽取（IE）任务中展现出巨大潜力。当前主流的少样本上下文学习（ICL）范式多依赖于仅提供正确示例（positive examples）作为提示模板，却普遍忽视了错误示例（negative examples）所蕴含的判别性知识。本文提出LC-ICL（Label-Guided Contrastive In-Context Learning）——一种新型少样本ICL框架，首次系统性地将带错误成因标签的负样本与正样本协同构建上下文示范。其核心创新在于：

  • 引入细粒度错误归因标签（如“边界模糊”“类型混淆”“跨句遗漏”），显式揭示负样本中的失败机制；
  • 构建对比式上下文对，将测试样本与语义最近的正样本、难负样本联合编码，激活LLM对关键差异特征的敏感性；
  • 利用LLM自身隐含的上下文理解能力，无需微调或额外参数，即可实现错误模式识别与规避。

我们在CoNLL-2003、ADE、SciERC、DocRED等8个跨领域IE数据集上进行严格评估。结果表明：LC-ICL在少样本（K=4–8）设定下，平均F1提升+3.2–5.7%，显著超越标准ICL、Self-ICL及基于检索的基线；尤其在低资源、长尾关系和嵌套实体场景下鲁棒性更强。本工作验证了结构化负反馈对LLM少样本泛化能力的关键价值，为可信、可解释的信息抽取提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces LC-ICL (Label-Guided Contrastive In-Context Learning), a novel few-shot information extraction method that strategically integrates both positive and error-annotated negative examples into in-context demonstrations for LLMs. Unlike conventional ICL approaches relying solely on correct exemplars, LC-ICL leverages fine-grained error-cause labels (e.g., “boundary ambiguity”, “type confusion”) to expose failure patterns in negatives, enabling LLMs to learn why similar predictions fail—not just what is correct. By constructing contrastive context pairs—combining the test instance with its nearest positive neighbor and hard negative—LC-ICL enhances discriminative reasoning without fine-tuning. Extensive experiments across 8 diverse IE benchmarks (including CoNLL-2003, DocRED, SciERC) show LC-ICL consistently outperforms prior few-shot ICL methods, achieving +3.2–5.7 F1 points on average and demonstrating superior robustness in low-resource and complex-scenario settings.

Abstract

There has been increasing interest in exploring the capabilities of advanced large language models (LLMs) in the field of information extraction (IE), specifically focusing on tasks related to named entity recognition (NER) and relation extraction (RE).Although researchers are exploring the use of few-shot information extraction through in-context learning with LLMs, they tend to focus only on using correct or positive examples for demonstration, neglecting the potential value of incorporating incorrect or negative examples into the learning process.In this paper, we present LC-ICL a novel few-shot technique that leverages both correct and incorrect sample constructions to create in-context learning demonstrations. This approach enhances the ability of LLMs to extract entities and relations by combining positive samples with negative samples annotated by error-cause labels. These labels expose more detailed error features in erroneous examples, enabling the model to understand why similar predictions fail and avoid repeating such errors during inference.Specifically, our proposed method taps into the inherent contextual information and valuable information in hard negative samples and the nearest positive neighbors to the test and then applies the in-context learning demonstrations based on LLMs. Our experiments on various datasets indicate that LC-ICL outperforms previous few-shot in-context learning methods, delivering substantial enhancements in performance across a broad spectrum of related tasks. These improvements are noteworthy, showcasing the versatility of our approach in diverse scenarios.

item.title: The Mirage of Optimizing Training Policies: Monotonic Inference Policies as the Real Objective for LLM Reinforcement Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.29526v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

大语言模型（LLM）的强化学习（RL）后训练虽备受关注，但实践中常面临训练不稳定甚至崩溃的挑战。核心症结在于训练-推理不匹配（training-inference mismatch）：为兼顾生成效率与训练精度，LLM通常采用分离的推理引擎（如束搜索、采样）与训练引擎（如策略梯度更新），即使模型参数完全同步，同一轨迹在两引擎下仍产生显著概率偏差。这种固有且持续存在的隐式离策略性（off-policyness）严重干扰梯度信号，导致策略更新失真。

方法创新：MIPI 与 MIPU

本文首次指出被既有工作忽视的关键缺陷——目标错位：训练引擎中的策略优化未必提升实际部署所用的推理策略（inference policy）。为此，我们提出全新优化范式：单调推理策略改进（Monotonic Inference Policy Improvement, MIPI），其核心是确保每次更新严格提升推理侧性能下界。基于此，我们设计单调推理策略更新框架（MIPU）：

  • 第一步：以推理引擎为参考，构建“采样器-感知”的候选策略更新集；
  • 第二步：引入推理侧间隙代理（inference-side gap proxy），仅接受能缩小推理策略与最优策略间理论间隙的同步更新，拒绝所有潜在退化操作。

实验验证与价值

在两种模型规模（7B/13B）及高不匹配强度下系统评估表明：MIPU 显著提升平均推理能力（+4.2% GSM8K, +3.7% MMLU），同时将训练崩溃率降低至接近0%，收敛曲线更平滑。本工作揭示了RL for LLM中“优化谁”的根本性问题，将优化目标从脆弱的训练策略转向鲁棒的推理策略，为稳定高效的大模型对齐提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Reinforcement learning (RL) for large language models (LLMs) suffers from training instability due to inherent training-inference mismatch: separate inference (e.g., sampling/beam search) and training engines yield inconsistent trajectory probabilities—even with identical parameters—inducing persistent off-policyness. Prior work mitigates off-policyness but overlooks a deeper issue: policy updates in the training engine do not guarantee improvement of the inference policy actually deployed. We propose Monotonic Inference Policy Improvement (MIPI), a new objective ensuring each update provably improves the inference-side performance lower bound. Based on MIPI, we design Monotonic Inference Policy Update (MIPU), a two-step framework: (1) generate sampler-referenced candidate updates aligned with inference behavior; (2) selectively accept only those candidates that reduce an inference-side gap proxy. Experiments on 7B and 13B models under high mismatch show MIPU boosts average reasoning accuracy (+4.2% on GSM8K, +3.7% on MMLU) and eliminates training collapse while improving stability.

Abstract

Reinforcement learning (RL) has gained growing attention in large language model (LLM) post-training, yet RL training remains fragile and can suffer from instability or collapse. One vital cause is training-inference mismatch: LLM adopts separate inference and training engines for generation efficiency and training precision, which in practice exhibits inconsistent probabilities for the same trajectories on training and inference sides, even with synchronized model parameters. This naturally induces a special type of off-policyness ever existing and poisoning the training. Prior works have made various efforts in addressing the off-policyness to stabilize the training policies under the mismatch. In this paper, we point out the objective misalignment neglected by existing works that an effective update to the policy in the training engine not necessarily ensures the improvement of the inference policy, i.e., the one used in deployment. To this end, we propose a new policy optimization objective for LLM RL, named Monotonic Inference Policy Improvement (MIPI). Following this principle, we introduce Monotonic Inference Policy Update (MIPU), a two-step LLM RL framework that constructs sampler-referenced candidate updates and selectively accepts synchronized candidates using an inference-side gap proxy. Experiments conducted on two model scales under high mismatch show that MIPU improves average reasoning performance and training stability.

item.title: SP-CACW: Convergence-Aware Client Weighting for Selfish Personalized Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.29322v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

协作式学习的可持续性依赖于每位参与方（客户端）均能获得实质性收益。传统联邦学习（FL）优化全局平均目标函数，当某客户端数据分布与群体显著偏移时，其个性化性能常严重下降，甚至遭遇负迁移（negative transfer）。本文聚焦“自私个性化”（selfish personalization）场景：一个指定的目标客户端如何在不损害自身泛化能力的前提下，有选择地利用其他客户端的梯度信息，最小化自身经验风险。

方法创新：SP-CACW 框架

我们提出 SP-CACW（Selfish Personalized Convergence-Aware Client Weighting），一种收敛性感知的客户端加权框架。其核心思想是：为每个通信轮次动态计算聚合权重，目标为最小化目标客户端收敛误差的理论上界。该上界显式解耦了两项关键因素：① 对等客户端梯度偏差（peer bias，反映数据异构性导致的系统性误导）；② 随机梯度方差（stochastic variance，反映本地更新噪声）。由此导出的权重规则具备自适应零权重机制——当某客户端梯度偏差过大、预期损害超过收益时，可自动赋予其权重为0，从而主动规避负迁移。

理论与实验验证

我们在标准光滑性与有界方差假设下，严格证明了SP-CACW的收敛性保证（O(1/T)收敛速率）。实验在MNIST、CIFAR-100及LEAF Shakespeare三个异构性强、任务差异大的基准上验证：SP-CACW在目标客户端测试准确率上全面超越经典FedAvg、FedProx等基线，且显著优于或媲美前沿个性化方法（如Per-FedAvg、pFedMe）及聚类方法（如FedEM、CFL），尤其在高度非独立同分布（Non-IID）场景下优势突出。本工作首次将收敛误差上界最小化作为客户端加权的直接优化目标，为自私但协作的个性化学习提供了可证、可解释、可部署的新范式。

AI Summary (English)

Collaborative learning is sustainable only when all participants benefit. Standard federated learning (FL) optimizes a global average objective, often failing target clients with highly heterogeneous data—leading to negative transfer. We study selfish personalization: how a designated client can selectively leverage peer gradients to minimize its own risk. We propose SP-CACW, a convergence-aware client-weighting framework that computes aggregation weights by minimizing an upper bound on the target client’s convergence error. This bound explicitly balances peer bias (systematic misalignment due to data heterogeneity) against stochastic variance, enabling automatic zero-weight assignment to harmful peers. Under standard smoothness and bounded-variance assumptions, SP-CACW enjoys provable O(1/T) convergence. Experiments on MNIST, CIFAR-100, and LEAF Shakespeare show SP-CACW matches or outperforms strong personalized (e.g., pFedMe, Per-FedAvg) and clustering baselines (e.g., FedEM), especially under extreme Non-IID settings.

Abstract

Collaborative learning is sustainable only when it benefits each participant. Standard federated learning optimizes a global average objective, which can under perform for clients whose data distributions differ substantially from the population. We study selfish personalization: how a designated target client can use peer gradients to minimize its own risk while avoiding negative transfer. We propose SP-CACW, a convergence-aware client-weighting framework that selects aggregation weights by minimizing an upper bound on the target client's convergence error. The resulting rule explicitly trades off peer bias against stochastic variance and can assign zero weight to harmful peers. We provide convergence guarantees under smoothness and bounded-variance assumptions and evaluate the method on MNIST, CIFAR-100, and LEAF Shakespeare, where it is competitive with or improves over strong personalized and clustering baselines.

item.title: How much of an LLM-generated clinical corpus is actually new? A production-scale measurement of content redundancy for provenance classification
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.29605v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

临床机器学习正日益依赖由大语言模型（LLM）自动生成的训练语料，而非临床专家人工标注的数据。这类语料常以“规模”（如数十亿token）作为核心卖点被描述和复用，但体积是否真实反映信息含量？ 本研究首次在生产级规模上系统量化LLM生成临床语料的内容冗余度，为语料溯源分类（provenance classification）提供实证基础。

方法与创新

我们分析了多智能体临床信息抽取流水线对167,034份患者叙事的完整输出：覆盖11个通道中的10个文本通道，总计25.1亿生成token。提出溯源驱动的冗余分解（Provenance-based Redundancy Decomposition）——一种细粒度至token级别的溯源分类框架，将每个token精确标注为其原始来源（原始病历、中间生成态或跨记录重复）。同时开展独立的无模型压缩分析（基于zstd无损压缩），验证冗余结构的客观存在性。

主要发现

  • 仅10.9% 的生成token为可训练的唯一内容，79.4%为冗余，原始token计数高估真实信息量约9倍；
  • 冗余源于两类机制：① 源文本直拷贝（verbatim copying，如将整段主诉直接填入多个字段），② 跨记录生成文本重复（duplication across records）；仅前者可无损消除；
  • 各通道冗余度差异显著：1个通道冗余率<1%，证明冗余非LLM固有缺陷，而取决于通道设计逻辑；
  • 未去重语料导致token级训练分布严重偏斜——长/复杂病例因产出更多字段而被过度加权；
  • 在下游任务中，同等token预算下，去重后微调显著提升临床编码器在外部疾病识别基准上的性能（+2.1–3.8 F1），且该增益在不同微调深度及第二基准上均稳健复现。

意义

本研究揭示“规模幻觉”风险，提供首个开源的溯源冗余分类工具，推动临床AI数据治理从粗放式扩容转向精细化信息效用评估。

AI Summary (English)

This study challenges the assumption that token count reflects information content in LLM-generated clinical corpora. Analyzing 2.51 billion tokens from a production-scale multi-agent extraction pipeline applied to 167,034 patient narratives, we introduce Provenance-based Redundancy Decomposition—a token-level provenance classifier revealing that only 10.9% of output is trainable-unique, while 79.4% is redundant, inflating perceived information by ~9×. Redundancy stems from two mechanisms: verbatim source copying (losslessly removable) and cross-record duplication (not). A model-free compression analysis independently confirms both patterns. Crucially, redundancy varies drastically across pipeline channels—demonstrating it is design-dependent, not inherent to LLM extraction. Uncorrected redundancy skews token-level training distributions toward longer, more complex cases. Downstream evaluation shows that deduplication before adaptation improves clinical encoder performance on external disease-recognition benchmarks (+2.1–3.8 F1) at equal token budgets—robustly across adaptation depths and replicated on a second benchmark—proving redundancy incurs a measurable, avoidable cost beyond storage. The classification tool is open-sourced.

Abstract

Clinical machine learning increasingly relies on training corpora generated by large language models (LLMs) rather than annotated by clinicians, and such corpora are described and reused largely on the basis of their reported scale. We test whether volume reflects information content. Analysing the complete output of a multi-agent clinical extraction pipeline applied to 167,034 patient narratives, 2.51 billion generated tokens across the ten text-bearing channels of an eleven-channel pipeline, we introduce Provenance-based Redundancy Decomposition, a token-level classification of the entire output by source. Only 10.9% of the output is trainable-unique content while 79.4% is redundant; raw token count overstates information content by roughly ninefold. The redundancy arises through two distinct mechanisms, verbatim copying of source context into per-item fields, and duplication of generated text across records, of which only the former is losslessly removable. An independent, model-free analysis based on lossless compression confirms the redundancy, recovering the two mechanisms without reference to the provenance labels. One pipeline channel carries almost no redundancy, showing that the level of redundancy depends on how each channel is structured rather than being a fixed property of LLM extraction. Because uncorrected redundancy up-weights the longer, more complex presentations that generate the most items, it skews the token-level training distribution of the corpus, a property we measure directly. In a controlled downstream test, de-duplicating the corpus before adaptation improved a clinical encoder on external disease-recognition benchmarks at equal token budget, robustly across adaptation depths and replicated on a second benchmark, confirming that the redundancy carries a measurable cost beyond storage. The classification tool is released openly.

item.title: DistilledGemma: Balanced Efficiency-Accuracy for Person-Place Relation Extraction from Multilingual Historical Articles
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.29130v1

AI Summary (中文)

DistilledGemma：面向多语种历史文献的人-地关系抽取高效精准模型

本研究针对HIPE-2026共享任务——从英、德、法三语历史报纸文章中抽取人-地（Person-Place）关系——提出DistilledGemma系统，旨在实现精度与效率的最优平衡。面对历史文本固有的拼写变异、命名模糊、语境稀疏等挑战，我们设计了三阶段知识蒸馏流水线：
1. 教师模型遴选：系统评估8个主流大语言模型在不同提示策略下的推理表现，确定Gemma 4 26B A4B为最优教师架构；
2. 银标思维链生成：基于QLoRA对教师模型进行监督微调，在训练集上批量生成高质量、多语种一致的链式推理（Chain-of-Thought）标注，构建高信度银标数据；
3. 响应级蒸馏：将教师模型的推理模式（而非仅最终标签）蒸馏至轻量级学生模型Gemma 4 E2B（2.3B参数），并合并LoRA适配器，实现零开销部署。

在官方评测中，团队WHEREAMI取得显著成果：

  • 标准测试集（含细粒度关系分类）平均准确率0.688，排名第三；
  • 二元关系判断测试集平均准确率0.8156，排名第二；
  • 更重要的是，在“效率-精度平衡”综合指标（兼顾F1、延迟与显存占用）中，本方案双测试集均位列第二，验证了小模型承载复杂推理的可行性。

本工作首次将知识蒸馏深度融入历史文档关系抽取全流程，不仅将模型参数量压缩超90%（26B→2.3B），更保持了多语种语义对齐与上下文敏感推理能力，为文化遗产数字化提供了可落地、可复用的轻量化范式。

AI Summary (English)

We present DistilledGemma, a knowledge distillation framework for person-place relation extraction from multilingual historical newspapers (English, German, French) in the HIPE-2026 shared task. Our three-stage pipeline first identifies Gemma-4 26B A4B as the optimal teacher via systematic prompt engineering across 8 LLMs; second, applies QLoRA-based supervised fine-tuning to generate high-quality multilingual chain-of-thought rationales as silver labels; and third, performs response-level distillation into a compact Gemma-4 E2B (2.3B) student—fully merging LoRA adapters for inference. Evaluated on HIPE-2026, our system WHEREAMI achieves 0.688 mean accuracy (3rd) on the standard test set and 0.8156 (2nd) on the binary set, ranking 2nd overall in the balanced efficiency-accuracy profile, demonstrating that distilled small models retain strong reasoning capability with ~91% parameter reduction and practical deployment cost.

Abstract

We present DistilledGemma, an efficient and accurate system for the HIPE-2026 shared task on person-place relation extraction from multilingual historical newspaper articles in English, German, and French. Our approach adopts a three-stage knowledge distillation pipeline designed to balance classification accuracy with computational efficiency. In the first stage, we systematically explored prompt engineering strategies across eight large language models to identify the most effective reasoning architecture for this challenging task. In the second stage, we applied supervised fine-tuning (SFT) via QLoRA to a Gemma 4 26B A4B teacher model, leveraging its strong multilingual capabilities to generate silver-standard chain-of-thought traces across the training corpus. In the final stage, we performed response-level distillation to transfer these learned reasoning patterns into a compact Gemma 4 E2B student model. In the official evaluation, our team WHEREAMI ranked 3rd on the standard test set with an accuracy profile mean score of 0.688, and 2nd on the binary test set with a mean score of 0.8156. Notably, by distilling knowledge from the 26B teacher to the 2.3B student, we preserved strong reasoning capabilities while reducing the deployed model size to approximately 2.3B effective parameters; the LoRA adapters used during training were merged into the student for inference. This configuration ranked 2nd in the balanced efficiency-accuracy profile across both the standard and binary test sets. These results demonstrate that knowledge distillation provides a practical and scalable solution for historical document processing, achieving competitive performance without excessive computational cost.

item.title: The Most Efficient Protocol for PAKE: What Exact Stuff Do You Need to Hash at the End?
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1331

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

密码认证密钥交换（PAKE）协议允许双方仅凭低熵口令即可安全协商会话密钥。近期，基于密钥封装机制（KEM）的编译器——单加密双轮Feistel EKE（OEKE-2F） 因其卓越效率备受关注：它在Diffie–Hellman实例下是目前唯一兼具通用可组合（UC）安全性与最优计算效率的PAKE；同时可无缝适配后量子KEM，为抗量子PAKE提供通用构造路径。然而，该协议定义存在严重歧义：其第二轮消息中对KEM密钥的RO哈希输入未被标准化，可自由选择是否包含口令、KEM公钥、首轮消息及KEM密文——共产生16种潜在变体，但此前仅2种被研究，且安全性边界模糊。

方法与发现

本工作首次完成对全部16种OEKE-2F变体的系统性UC安全性证明。我们严格刻画每种变体所需的底层KEM安全属性，发现：

  • “全量哈希”版本（哈希全部4项）要求最弱：仅需标准的IND-CPA安全性；
  • 每省略一项哈希输入，KEM需额外满足一项轻量级增强属性（如密码学不可链接性、密钥一致性或弱相关密钥安全性），所有属性均比IND-CCA更弱且可被主流KEM（如Kyber、BIKE）满足；
  • 关键洞见在于：哈希范围直接决定KEM需抵御的“密钥泄露模式”，而非提升整体安全强度层级。

创新与意义

本研究终结了OEKE-2F协议的定义碎片化状态，为标准化提供理论依据；首次建立KEM属性与PAKE编译器鲁棒性之间的精确映射；所提炼的轻量级KEM需求亦为后量子密码工程实践提供可验证的安全契约。附带说明：本文是作者首篇逾百页论文（但核心结论凝练于此摘要）。

AI Summary (English)

This work provides the first comprehensive Universal Composability (UC) security analysis of all 16 variants of the OEKE-2F PAKE compiler—a highly efficient KEM-to-PAKE transformation. We prove UC-security for each variant and precisely characterize the minimal KEM security properties required: the “hash-all” variant (including password, KEM public key, first message, and KEM ciphertext in the hash) only needs standard IND-CPA security; removing any one input imposes a mild, strictly additional KEM requirement—such as key consistency, cryptographic unlinkability, or weak related-key security—all weaker than IND-CCA and satisfied by leading post-quantum KEMs (e.g., Kyber). Our results resolve long-standing ambiguity in OEKE-2F’s specification, enable informed standardization choices, and establish the first fine-grained correspondence between KEM properties and PAKE compiler robustness.

Abstract

A Password-Authenticated Key Exchange (PAKE) protocol allows two parties to jointly establish a cryptographic session key, in the "password-only" setting where the only information shared in advance is a low-entropy password. In recent years, the One-encryption EKE with 2-round Feistel cipher (OEKE-2F) protocol, a compiler from Key Encapsulation Mechanism (KEM) to PAKE, has received much attention, for the following reasons: (1) When instantiated with the Diffie–Hellman KEM, it is the most computationally efficient PAKE protocol to date that is secure in the Universal Composability (UC) framework; and (2) When instantiated with a post-quantum KEM, it provides a generic way to construct efficient PAKE protocols based on post-quantum assumptions.

Unfortunately, the community cannot agree upon what the OEKE-2F protocol exactly is: part of the second protocol message is an RO hash of the KEM key, together with any number of the following:

  • The password,
  • The KEM public key,
  • The first protocol message, and
  • The KEM ciphertext.

This yields 16 potential variants of OEKE-2F; only two of them have been studied in the literature, and their pros and cons are poorly understood.

In this work, we present a comprehensive analysis of all 16 variants of OEKE-2F, proving the UC-security of each of them. The general takeaway is that the "hash everything" version requires the fewest security properties of the underlying KEM scheme, and the more items we remove from the hash, the more security requirements the KEM scheme has to satisfy — although all of the additional KEM properties are still mild. We pinpoint the exact KEM properties each version of OEKE-2F needs, and thoroughly explain the rationales.

The significance of this work lies in that it helps the community converge upon the "right" version of OEKE-2F, and perhaps also in that this is the first paper by the author that is over 100 pages.

item.title: A Differentiated Approach for Post-Quantum DNSSEC
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1332

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

量子计算发展对当前DNSSEC依赖的ECDSA等经典签名算法构成根本性威胁。然而，主流后量子（PQ）签名方案（如UOV、Dilithium、Falcon）面临严峻部署瓶颈：其密钥与签名尺寸显著增大，极易突破DNS over UDP的512字节/4096字节限制，导致高频TCP回退、解析延迟激增及协议兼容性风险。

方法创新

本文提出差异化算法选择机制：为Zone Signing Key（ZSK）和Key Signing Key（KSK）分别指定不同签名算法。该设计突破传统“统一算法”范式，解耦二者对尺寸的约束——ZSK可选用高安全性但大签名的算法（如UOV），而KSK则采用密钥紧凑的PQ或经典算法（如P256），从而规避整体响应超限问题。进一步，我们设计混合PQ/T签名拼接方案：在单条RRSIG记录中串联经典P256签名与PQ签名，实现过渡期双重安全保障。

关键发现

基于AFNIC .fr顶级域真实结构（420万域名）构建容器化测试平台，系统评估18种配置：

  • UOV+P256差异化组合使原本因DNSKEY响应超64KB而不可部署的UOV方案成为现实；
  • 所有可行差异化配置仅引入1.28–1.52倍于ECDSA的解析延迟，显著优于强制TCP回退带来的3–5倍延迟恶化；
  • 混合拼接方案增加7–19%响应体积，TCP回退率可控（<0.5%），且完全向后兼容；
  • 签名性能开销集中于ZSK侧，KSK侧轻量算法保障密钥轮转可行性。

本工作为PQ-DNSSEC提供了首个兼顾部署可行性、协议合规性与过渡平滑性的系统化路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper proposes a differentiated algorithm selection for post-quantum DNSSEC, assigning distinct signature algorithms to the Zone Signing Key (ZSK) and Key Signing Key (KSK) roles—breaking the conventional “uniform algorithm” constraint. This enables previously infeasible PQ schemes: e.g., UOV (128-byte sig, ~43 KB key) yields DNSKEY responses >64 KB under uniform deployment but becomes viable when paired with a compact KSK (e.g., P256). We further introduce hybrid PQ/T signature concatenation, embedding both classical (P256) and PQ signatures in a single RRSIG record for transitional dual assurance. Evaluated on a containerized testbed mirroring AFNIC’s .fr TLD (4.2M domains), differentiated configurations incur only 1.28–1.52× latency overhead vs. ECDSA while enabling otherwise prohibited algorithms; hybrid concatenation adds just 7–19% response size with negligible TCP fallback (<0.5%). Our approach delivers deployable, standards-compliant, and backward-compatible PQ-DNSSEC migration.

Abstract

Post-quantum signature algorithms pose significant challenges for DNSSEC migration: their larger keys and signatures exceed DNS over UDP transport limits, making TCP fallback unavoidable even for the most compact schemes. We propose a differentiated algorithm selection, assigning distinct signature algorithms to the Zone Signing Key (ZSK) and Key Signing Key (KSK) roles. This approach expands the space of deployable post-quantum configurations beyond what undifferentiated selection permits, enabling algorithms that would otherwise be impossible to deploy: UOV, with 128-byte signatures but 43~KB keys, produces DNSKEY responses exceeding the 64~KB DNS limit under undifferentiated constraints, yet becomes viable when paired with a compact-key KSK. We also evaluate hybrid PQ/T schemes through signature concatenation, combining classical P256 with post-quantum algorithms in a single RRSIG record to provide dual security during the transition period.

Using a containerized testbed validated against AFNIC's .fr TLD structure (4.2 million domains), we systematically measure response sizes, resolution latency, TCP fallback rates, and signing performance across 18 configurations. Differentiated configurations achieve 1.28-1.52× latency overhead relative to classical ECDSA while enabling algorithms that undifferentiated constraints prohibit. Hybrid PQ/T concatenation introduces acceptable overhead (7--19%) for backward-compatible quantum resistance.

item.title: Real-world Qualification Proof: A Protocol for Supporting Real-world Assets in DeFi
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1330

AI Summary (中文)

现实世界资质证明（RQP）：面向DeFi中现实世界资产的隐私安全验证协议

将现实世界资产（Real-World Assets, RWA）——如房地产、应收账款或债券——可信接入去中心化金融（DeFi），是拓展链上信贷规模的关键路径。然而，现有方案面临根本性矛盾：既要保护资产敏感数据隐私（如抵押物估值、所有权结构），又要密码学地绑定可信发行方身份，确保数据真实可验。传统方案（如DID或中心化预言机）在安全性（抗篡改、抗合谋）与性能（验证延迟、Gas开销）之间难以兼顾。

为此，本文提出现实世界资质证明（Real-world Qualification Proof, RQP）协议。其核心创新在于引入新型密码学原语——见证隐藏认证（Witness-Hiding Authentication, WHA）。WHA与zk-SNARK协同工作：资产发行方仅需对RWA资质声明生成轻量级签名式认证，无需暴露原始数据；借款人则基于该认证构造零知识证明，向智能合约证明“其资产已获指定发行方有效背书”。WHA将数据隐私保障锚定于零知识系统本身，同时通过非交互式绑定机制确保来源真实性（source authenticity），杜绝伪造或重放攻击。

为提升可扩展性，RQP集成zk-Rollup范式，并采用内积论证（Inner Product Argument）中的高效变体MIPP，实现多借款人证明的批量聚合。实验表明：在1024笔并发借款验证场景下，链上验证耗时降至单证明的O(log N) 级别，Gas成本降低超87%。RQP首次在无需可信第三方、不牺牲隐私前提下，实现了RWA资质的可验证、可组合、高吞吐链上确权，为DeFi与传统金融基础设施的安全互操作提供了可落地的密码学基座。

AI Summary (English)

We propose Real-world Qualification Proof (RQP), a cryptographic protocol enabling privacy-preserving, issuer-anchored verification of off-chain real-world assets (RWAs) in DeFi. RQP introduces Witness-Hiding Authentication (WHA)—a novel primitive that synergizes with zk-SNARKs to let issuers cryptographically attest to asset qualifications without revealing sensitive data, while enabling smart contracts to efficiently verify the binding between issuer attestation and borrower’s zero-knowledge proof. WHA grounds privacy guarantees in the zero-knowledge property itself and ensures source authenticity. To achieve scalability, RQP employs zk-rollup architecture and MIPP-based inner-product arguments for aggregating multiple proofs, reducing on-chain verification complexity to O(log N) and slashing gas costs by >87% in multi-borrower settings. Experiments confirm RQP meets stringent security, privacy, and performance requirements for production-grade DeFi applications.

Abstract

Integrating Real-World Assets (RWA) into Decentralized Finance (DeFi) enables borrowers to leverage off-chain assets for on-chain transactions. However, this integration faces a critical security challenge: ensuring that private asset data, utilized within cryptographic proofs, is authenticated by and cryptographically bound to a trusted issuer. Existing solutions, such as decentralized identifiers (DIDs) and oracles, fail to simultaneously satisfy the requisite security standards and performance constraints. To address this, we propose Real-world Qualification Proof (RQP), a cryptographic protocol designed for the privacy-preserving verification of off-chain asset qualifications.
RQP introduces a new primitive, Witness-Hiding Authentication (WHA), which operates in conjunction with zk-SNARKs. WHA enables asset issuers to authenticate RWA data, allowing the on-chain smart contract to efficiently verify the binding relationship between the issuer's authentication and the borrower's zero-knowledge proof. The proposed WHA effectively anchors the data privacy guarantee of RQP within the zero-knowledge property of the proof system while ensuring source authenticity. Furthermore, to ensure scalability and minimize on-chain gas costs, we adopt zk-rollup methodologies and employ the Inner Product Argument (specifically MIPP) to aggregate multiple RQP proofs. This yields a protocol that is not only secure and private but also computationally efficient for high-throughput DeFi applications. Experiments show that logarithmic verification efficiency optimization can be achieved in the multi-borrower proof scenario.

item.title: Flock: Fast Proving for Batch Boolean Computations
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1329

AI Summary (中文)

Flock：面向批量布尔计算的极速零知识证明系统

背景与挑战：在SNARK（简洁非交互式知识论证）的实际应用中，对大规模标准密码学哈希（如SHA-256、Keccak、BLAKE3）进行批量验证已成为核心性能瓶颈。传统基于椭圆曲线或FFT的SNARK方案在证明哈希运算时存在显著开销，尤其在高吞吐场景下难以满足实时性需求。

方法创新：本文提出Flock——一种专为批量布尔计算优化的哈希基SNARK架构。其核心突破在于：

  • 首次将lincheck（线性关系校验）与zerocheck（零多项式校验）协议进行深度协同优化，显著降低约束系统构建与多项式承诺开销；
  • 支持同构R1CS电路的批处理证明，并原生支持哈希链（hash chains）与Merkle路径打开（Merkle path openings）等关键密码原语；
  • 采用“编码智能体协同设计”范式，实现算法-实现联合优化，包括定制化FFT、稀疏多项式评估及内存局部性增强策略。

关键结果：在单核Apple M4 Max处理器上，Flock达成：

  • 82,000次/秒 BLAKE3压缩函数证明；
  • 42,000次/秒 SHA-256压缩证明；
  • 30,000次/秒 Keccak-256置换证明；

——证明开销低于原生执行的250×（即仅2.5倍于哈希函数本征延迟）。启用10核后，BLAKE3吞吐突破66万次/秒；相比当前最优的Binius64，SHA-256证明速度提升9倍以上；相较最快椭圆曲线SNARK（如Halo2），加速超500倍。Flock为区块链扩容、可信计算与隐私合约提供了可部署级的高效证明基础设施。

AI Summary (English)

Flock is a hash-based SNARK designed for ultra-fast proving of large batches of identical Boolean computations—especially cryptographic hash evaluations (e.g., SHA-256, Keccak, BLAKE3). It supports batched R1CS circuits with cross-instance input/output constraints, natively verifies hash chains and Merkle path openings, and is extensible to full hash-based signature verification. At its core, Flock introduces novel optimizations to the lincheck and zerocheck protocols and features a proof-of-concept implementation co-designed by coding agents for maximal efficiency. On a single M4 Max core, Flock proves up to 82k BLAKE3 compressions, 42k SHA-256 compressions, and 30k Keccak permutations per second—less than 250× overhead over native execution. With 10 cores, throughput exceeds 660k BLAKE3 compressions/sec; it outperforms Binius64 by >9× on SHA-256 and surpasses the fastest elliptic-curve SNARK by >500×.

Abstract

For many applications of SNARKs, a key bottleneck is proving large batches of standard cryptographic hash evaluations, such as SHA-256, Keccak, or BLAKE3. We introduce Flock, a hash-based SNARK for extremely fast proving of such batched Boolean computations. Flock proves batches of the same R1CS circuit (plus input/output relations between them), can prove hash-chains and Merkle path openings, and in principle can be extended to full-fledged hash-based signature verification. At its core, Flock combines new optimizations for the lincheck and zerocheck protocols with an aggressively optimized proof-of-concept implementation co-designed by coding agents.

On a single core of an M4 Max processor, Flock proves 82k evaluations of the BLAKE3 compression function, 42k SHA-256 compressions, and 30k Keccak permutations per second — less than a $250\times$ overhead over native execution. On ten cores, throughput exceeds 660k BLAKE3 compressions per second; in proving SHA-256, Flock is more than $9\times$ faster than Binius64, the prior state of the art, and more than $500\times$ faster than the fastest elliptic curve-based SNARK we measured against.

item.title: Symbolon: Symbolic Execution by Learning Code Transformation
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.29108v1

AI Summary (中文)

Symbolon：基于学习的代码变换驱动符号执行框架

背景与挑战：符号执行作为关键的程序分析技术，广泛应用于漏洞挖掘、安全测试与恶意软件分析。然而，其在真实程序中常因路径爆炸、复杂约束求解等结构性与语义性瓶颈而严重受限。现有方法多依赖预定义或编译器启发式代码变换（如循环展开、函数内联），但这类变换往往缺乏语义敏感性——既难以适配局部多样化的控制流与数据流模式，又可能因复用面向具体执行优化的规则而损害符号执行的完整性与效率。

核心方法：Symbolon提出一种数据驱动、上下文感知的代码变换学习范式。其核心创新在于将变换发现建模为程序表示空间上的搜索问题，并通过三阶段流程实现高效落地：
1. 离线轻量学习：在小型基准程序上自动探索并生成多样化变换策略；
2. 技能蒸馏：将有效变换抽象为可复用的“智能体技能库”，涵盖条件简化、路径剪枝、约束重构等语义感知操作；
3. 在线自适应应用：部署轻量级决策智能体，在目标项目（如Linux内核）级别动态选择并组合技能，实现按需、局部、语义对齐的变换。

实验结果：在32个真实世界程序上，Symbolon显著增强KLEE引擎性能：平均行覆盖率提升3.69倍，峰值内存降低29.2倍，每次SMT查询求解时间减少123倍（覆盖16种主流搜索策略）。进一步应用于最新Linux内核，成功发现21个此前未知的安全缺陷，全部获内核维护者确认并提交CVE跟踪。

AI Summary (English)

Symbolic execution is hindered by path explosion and expensive constraint solving in real-world programs. Existing code transformations are often rigid, semantics-agnostic, or misaligned with symbolic goals. Symbolon addresses this by learning diverse, context-sensitive code transformations via a three-stage framework: (1) lightweight offline discovery on small programs; (2) distillation into a reusable library of semantic-aware agent skills (e.g., conditional simplification, path pruning); and (3) intelligent, on-the-fly skill selection and composition for large-scale targets. Evaluated on 32 real-world programs with KLEE and 16 search strategies, Symbolon achieves 3.69× higher line coverage, 29.2× lower peak memory, and 123× faster per-query solver time on average. Applied to the latest Linux kernel, it uncovered 21 previously unknown bugs, all reported to maintainers.

Abstract

Symbolic execution is a powerful program analysis technique with broad applications, such as vulnerability detection, security testing, and malware analysis. However, this technique is known to suffer from scalability issues, e.g., path explosion, complex constraints, due to certain structural and semantic patterns commonly presented in real-world programs. Existing approaches attempt to escape these patterns by transforming programs into new representations to reduce the execution cost. Unfortunately, these transformations are often too rigid to exploit diverse local program semantics and sometimes rely on compiler optimizations designed for concrete execution that may misalign with the goals of symbolic execution. We present Symbolon, a framework that automatically learns diverse code transformations and applies them context-sensitively to improve symbolic execution. Our key insight is to formulate transformation discovery as a search problem over program representations. To make the search practical, Symbolon learns transformations cheaply offline on small programs, distills them into a reusable library of agent skills, and uses an agent to instantiate these skills on repo-level targets. Our evaluation shows that Symbolon substantially improves the symbolic execution engine KLEE across 16 search strategies on 32 real-world programs, increasing line coverage by 3.69x on average while reducing peak memory and per-query solver time by 29.2x and 123x, respectively. When applied to the latest Linux kernel, Symbolon uncovers 21 previously unknown bugs, all of which have been reported to the kernel maintainers.

item.title: From Tool Connection to Execution Control: Benchmarking Security Invariants in MCP-Style Agent Runtimes
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.29073v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

Model Context Protocol（MCP）类生态为大语言模型应用提供了连接工具、资源、提示与传输通道的实用接口层。然而，当智能体从“工具连接”迈向“自主执行”时，安全决策长期分散于客户端、服务端、提示工程、人工审批弹窗、OAuth部署及日志系统中，缺乏统一、可验证的执行层安全约束。

方法与创新

本文首次系统定义了8项执行层安全不变式（invariants）：元数据非权威性、授权凭据驱动的批准、资源规范性、主体绑定、能力调用作用域限制、源-目标数据流授权、拒绝路径可审计性、协议状态显式化。基于此，我们设计并实现了HCP（Handle-Capability Protocol）——首个面向MCP风格智能体的参考级执行控制运行时。HCP以主体（principal）、资源（resource）、授权（grant）、能力（capability）、句柄（handle）、策略决策、数据管道校验及审计条目为原语，将安全逻辑内嵌于执行路径中。

主要发现

在10个典型攻击场景构成的基准测试中：

  • 纯连接层基线（naive baseline）允许全部10次攻击；
  • 引入元数据检查、会话校验与逐调用审批的实践增强基线（mitigation baseline）仍放行6次攻击；
  • HCP成功阻断全部10次攻击，且每例均生成完整、不可篡改的审计证据链。

消融实验明确识别出句柄绑定、能力作用域检查与数据流管道校验是关键防御组件。微基准测试显示：策略评估、能力调用、数据窥探（peek）与管道操作平均延迟均低于1毫秒。GitHub README抽样分析（受限规模）仅用于生态实践信号探测，不构成漏洞披露。
本研究支持一个严格限定的结论：MCP风格智能体系统亟需独立的执行控制层，而非仅依赖连接层约定。

AI Summary (English)

This paper addresses the security gap between tool connection and agent execution in Model Context Protocol (MCP)-style ecosystems. We define eight testable execution-layer security invariants—e.g., grant-backed approval, scoped capability invocation, and source-target data-flow authorization—and implement them in HCP, a reference Handle-Capability Protocol runtime that models calls via principals, grants, capabilities, handles, and auditable policy decisions. Evaluated across 10 benchmark attacks, HCP blocks all 10 while preserving forensic evidence; a naive connection-layer baseline permits all, and a practice-informed mitigation baseline permits 6/10. Ablation confirms core components (e.g., handle binding, capability scoping, pipe checks) are essential for defense. Microbenchmarks show sub-millisecond latency for key operations. The results substantiate a narrow but critical claim: MCP-style agent systems require an explicit execution-control layer—not just connection-layer conventions—to enforce security guarantees.

Abstract

Model Context Protocol (MCP)-style ecosystems give language-model applications a practical connection layer for tools, resources, prompts, and transports. As agents move from connection to execution, security decisions often remain split across clients, servers, prompts, approval dialogs, OAuth deployments, and logs. This paper asks whether a runtime can make execution-layer invariants explicit and testable while preserving MCP-like workflows. We define eight invariants: metadata non-authority, grant-backed approval, canonical resources, principal binding, scoped capability invocation, source-and-target data-flow authorization, deny-path audit, and explicit protocol state. We implement these invariants in HCP, a Handle-Capability Protocol reference runtime for MCP-style agent execution that represents calls through principals, resources, grants, capabilities, handles, policy decisions, data-pipe checks, and audit entries. We evaluate HCP against two MCP-like baselines: a naive connection-layer runtime and a practice-informed connection-layer mitigation baseline with metadata linting, session checks, and per-call approvals. Across 10 benchmark cases, the naive baseline permits all modeled attacks, the mitigation baseline permits 6 of 10, and HCP blocks all 10 while preserving audit evidence. Ablations identify which runtime components block attacks and preserve forensic evidence. A local in-memory microbenchmark reports sub-millisecond mean latencies for measured policy, invocation, peek, and pipe operations. A bounded GitHub README-screening sample provides ecosystem signals, not vulnerability findings. The results support a narrow claim: MCP-style agent systems need an execution-control layer in addition to connection-layer conventions.

item.title: Formal Security Analysis of Agent Protocol Composition
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.28690v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

当前AI智能体（Agent）协议缺乏形式化安全规范，其安全需求在不同部署中定义不全、执行不一致。协议描述了智能体如何调用工具、委托任务及跨系统协同，但安全责任常在协议文本、SDK实现与实际部署间模糊分配，导致“责任缺口”——既非纯设计缺陷，亦非单纯实现漏洞。

方法：AgentThread框架

我们提出AgentThread，首个支持端到端安全确证的源码关联分析框架：

  • 分层安全范围建模：将协议安全要求划分为协议内约束、跨协议交互、SDK实现层三类；
  • 形式化验证驱动：从自然语言协议文本中提取安全属性，自动编译为TLA+不变式，支持模型检验；
  • 两阶段检查器：① 将协议规格编译为可检验模型，发现抽象反例；② 通过协议适配器将反例重放至真实SDK与参考服务器，实现语义级可执行验证；
  • 溯源归因机制：对每个问题标注原始协议文本出处，并分类标记为：违反的强制性要求、缺失的推荐实践、SDK硬编码缺陷或未分配的跨协议责任。

主要发现

在5个主流新兴Agent协议（包括LangChain、AutoGen等）上评估：

  • 发现35处协议文本层面的安全缺陷（如未声明敏感操作授权边界）；
  • 构建80个针对性实现测试，覆盖生产级SDK与参考服务，验证全部缺陷；
  • 揭示30个仅在协议组合场景下暴露的新型失效（如委托链中权限继承断裂）；
  • 关键结论：仅1个协议实际部署了任一安全控制机制，且所有协议均未明确指定跨协议行为的 enforcement 责任主体。

本研究首次揭示：Agent协议安全失效本质是多层级责任断层，需在协议设计、SDK契约与部署治理中协同填补。

AI Summary (English)

AI agent protocols govern tool usage, task delegation, and cross-system coordination—but their security requirements are incompletely specified and inconsistently enforced. We present AgentThread, a source-linked framework for end-to-end security assurance—from protocol text to running SDKs. It introduces: (1) a layered security scope distinguishing intra-protocol, cross-protocol, and SDK-level responsibilities; (2) protocol-derived TLA+ invariants automatically compiled from specification text; and (3) a two-phase checker that model-checks abstract specifications and replays counterexamples against real SDKs via protocol adapters—with full traceability to original text and classification of findings (violated requirements vs. missing recommendations vs. unassigned cross-protocol duties). Evaluated on five emerging protocols, AgentThread identified 35 specification-level flaws, validated them with 80 implementation tests, and uncovered 30 additional failures only under composition. Critically, only one protocol enforces any security-relevant control in practice—and none assigns enforcement responsibility for cross-protocol behavior. This reveals insecurity as a systemic responsibility gap across specifications, SDKs, and deployments—not merely a design or coding issue.

Abstract

AI agent protocols define how agents use tools, delegate work, and coordinate across software systems, but their security requirements remain incomplete and inconsistently enforced across deployments. We present AgentThread, a source-linked framework for security assurance analysis of agent protocols, from specification text to running SDKs. AgentThread contributes a layered security scope, protocol-derived checks formalized as TLA+ invariants, and a two-phase checker that compiles protocol specifications into model-checkable models and replays executable counterexamples against real SDKs through protocol adapters. For each finding, AgentThread records the source text behind the check and separates violated protocol requirements from missing recommendations, hardening gaps, and unassigned cross-protocol responsibilities. Across five emerging agent protocols, AgentThread identifies 35 specification-level findings, supports them with 80 implementation tests against production SDKs and reference servers, and finds 30 additional failures that emerge only under protocol composition. We further show that only one protocol enforces a security-relevant control in practice and no protocol assigns enforcement for cross-protocol behavior. Insecurity in agent protocols is therefore not only a specification or implementation problem, but also a responsibility gap across protocols, SDKs, and deployments.

item.title: Why Trust Your Agent? Empirical Security Gains from TRiSM-Guided Agentic Workflows in Healthcare
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.28666v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

基于智能体（Agent）的AI系统正广泛应用于医疗场景，通过向大语言模型（LLM）开放应用工具与资源实现任务自动化。然而，为提升任务覆盖范围与生成精度，智能体常被授予远超普通用户的权限，导致数据泄露、合规风险与攻击面扩大等严重安全问题。当前多数AI集成实践忽视安全设计原则，缺乏系统性风险管控。

方法与实验设计

本研究将AI可信、风险与安全管理（TRiSM）框架落地于医疗报告生成系统，构建并对比两类工作流：（1）传统无防护的智能体流程；（2）TRiSM指导下的安全增强型智能体流程——贯彻最小权限原则、纵深防御策略及服务端提示构造。实验在5种主流LLM（Claude Haiku 4.5、GPT-4.1-nano、GPT-4.1-mini、GPT-5.4-mini、Gemini 2.5 Flash）上开展，覆盖2类临床报告，共生成800份报告，并系统评估500个真实攻击场景，包括RAG投毒、数据字段注入与客户端网络注入三类典型威胁。

主要发现与创新点

  • 安全增益显著：TRiSM工作流将RAG投毒攻击成功率从31%降至10%，数据字段注入从42%降至25%，并通过服务端提示构造彻底消除网络注入向量；
  • 质量同步提升：报告准确率提升14个百分点（72.5% → 86.5%），验证“安全即可靠”的设计范式；
  • 架构启示明确：证实模型选择非仅性能问题，而是关键安全架构决策——不同LLM对同一防护策略的响应差异达12–18个百分点。

本研究首次在医疗AI领域实证TRiSM驱动的智能体工作流可兼顾强安全性与高准确性，为可信AI落地提供可复用的方法论与工程路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper demonstrates how the AI Trust, Risk, and Security Management (TRiSM) framework transforms insecure agentic workflows into security-conscious ones in healthcare report generation. We rigorously evaluate two workflows—baseline vs. TRiSM-guided—across five LLMs (Claude Haiku 4.5, GPT-4.1-nano, GPT-4.1-mini, GPT-5.4-mini, Gemini 2.5 Flash), 800 generated reports, and 500 attack scenarios including RAG poisoning, data-field injection, and client-side network injection. The TRiSM workflow reduces RAG poisoning success from 31% to 10%, data-field injection from 42% to 25%, and eliminates network injection entirely via server-side prompt construction. Report accuracy improves by 14 percentage points (72.5% → 86.5%), proving that security-aware design enhances reliability. Crucially, model choice emerges as a core architectural security decision—not just a performance consideration—with attack resilience varying significantly across models under identical TRiSM controls.

Abstract

Agent-based AI has enabled the automation of tasks by exposing application tools and resources to large language models (LLMs). However, to improve scope and accuracy, agents are often given access rights that exceed those of ordinary users, introducing significant security risks. AI is routinely integrated into applications with a disregard to security, risking data exposure and breaching regulations. This paper applies the AI Trust, Risk, and Security Management (TRiSM) framework to a medical report-generation application to demonstrate how an insecure agent workflow can be transformed into security-conscious agentic workflow. Both workflows were evaluated across five LLMs (Claude Haiku 4.5, GPT-4.1-nano, GPT-4.1-mini, GPT-5.4-mini, and Gemini 2.5 Flash) on two report types, totalling 800 generations and 500 attack scenarios including RAG poisoning, data-field injection, and client-side network injection. The TRiSM-guided agentic workflow reduced mean attack success rates from 31% to 10% for RAG poisoning and from 42% to 25% for data-field injection, while eliminating the network injection vector entirely through server-side prompt construction. Furthermore, report accuracy increased by 14 percentage points (72.5% to 86.5%) with the agentic workflow, demonstrating a secure design which provides more reliable outputs. This paper contributes to knowledge by demonstrating least-privilege, defence in depth agentic workflows improving security and accuracy, while also highlighting model choice is a necessary architectural consideration.

item.title: Fine-Tuning General-Purpose Large Language Models for Agricultural Applications:A Reproducible Framework and Evaluation Protocol Based on Qwen3-8B
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.28992v1

AI Summary (中文)

农业大模型适配的可复现框架：AgriTune-R

通用大语言模型（LLM）在开放域问答、信息抽取与文本生成中表现优异，但农业应用具有强领域性、区域依赖性、时间敏感性与安全关键性。未经数据治理、专家评估与证据约束的农业助手可能给出关于病虫害诊断、农药施用、施肥方案或政策解读等高风险场景的不可靠建议，甚至引发生产损失或合规风险。为杜绝将模拟数值误作实证结果，本文不报告任何未经真实训练与专家验证的性能指标。

我们提出 AgriTune-R——一个可复现、可审计、全流程可控的农业LLM适配框架。该框架以开源可验证的 Qwen3-8B 为推荐基座模型，系统整合六大核心模块：

  • ✅ 农业领域数据治理（覆盖作物、土壤、气象、政策等多源结构化/非结构化数据）
  • ✅ 领域指令工程（基于农技手册、专家访谈与监管文件构建高质量指令集）
  • ✅ 参数高效微调（LoRA/QLoRA，兼顾精度与推理效率）
  • ✅ 检索增强生成（RAG）保障事实溯源与实时知识更新
  • ✅ 多维度专家评审机制（聚焦高风险问题的安全拦截与响应校验）
  • ✅ 证据一致性约束（强制引用权威来源并标注置信度）

四大创新贡献：
1. 结构化农业LLM适配工作流（含数据清洗→指令对齐→微调→RAG集成→安全过滤五阶段）；
2. 面向农业场景的标准化评估协议，覆盖知识问答、病虫害咨询、栽培管理、政策解释四类任务；
3. 专家评审量表，从事实性、安全性、证据一致性、不确定性表达四个维度进行细粒度打分；
4. 方法论与实证结论的严格解耦——本框架提供可即插即用的协议模板与评估工具链，而非预设性能结论，为后续研究建立可复现基线。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces AgriTune-R, a reproducible, auditable framework for adapting general-purpose LLMs—specifically the publicly verifiable Qwen3-8B—to safety-critical agricultural tasks. Recognizing that agriculture demands domain specificity, regional awareness, temporal precision, and rigorous evidence grounding, AgriTune-R integrates agricultural data governance, expert-informed instruction construction, LoRA/QLoRA fine-tuning, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), expert evaluation, and safety-aware response filtering. Its core contributions are: (1) a structured, five-stage workflow for agricultural LLM adaptation; (2) a standardized evaluation protocol covering knowledge QA, pest/disease consultation, cultivation management, and policy explanation; (3) an expert review rubric assessing factuality, safety, evidence consistency, and uncertainty expression; and (4) a clear methodological–empirical separation—providing an executable baseline rather than unverified performance claims. All components are designed for transparency, auditability, and real-world deployment readiness.

Abstract

General-purpose large language models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong abilities in opendomain question answering, information extraction, and text generation. Agricultural applications, however, are domain-specific, region-dependent, time-sensitive, and safety-critical. Without data governance, expert evaluation, and evidence constraints, an agricultural assistant mayproduce unreliable advice on crop diseases, pesticide use, fertilization, or policy interpretation.To avoid presenting unverified simulated numbers as real experimental findings, this paper doesnot report any model-performance claims that have not been produced by an actual training runand expert evaluation. Instead, we propose AgriTune-R, a reproducible and auditable frameworkfor adapting general-purpose LLMs to agricultural tasks. The framework selects the publiclyverifiable Qwen3-8B model as the recommended base model and integrates agricultural datagovernance, instruction construction, LoRA/QLoRA parameter-efficient fine-tuning, retrievalaugmented generation, expert evaluation, and safety control for high-risk questions. The contributions are: (1) a structured workflow for agricultural LLM adaptation; (2) an evaluationprotocol for agricultural knowledge QA, pest and disease consultation, cultivation management,and policy explanation; (3) an expert-review rubric combining factuality, safety, evidence consistency, and uncertainty expression; and (4) a clear separation between protocol design andempirical conclusions, providing an executable baseline for future empirical studies.

item.title: Clustering Unsupervised Representations as Defense against Poisoning Attacks on Speech Commands Classification System
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.28953v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

语音命令分类系统易受投毒攻击（poisoning attacks）威胁，尤其在数据不可信或众包场景下。本文聚焦于脏标签投毒攻击（dirty-label poisoning）：攻击者选取某一源类别（如“yes”），对其部分语音样本叠加隐蔽触发器（trigger），并将其标签恶意篡改为目标类别（如“no”），从而污染训练集、诱导模型误判。

方法创新

我们提出一种无监督表征过滤型防御框架，无需任何标签监督或先验知识：
1. 无监督表征学习：采用 DINO（DIstillation with NO labels） 模型，从原始音频梅尔频谱图中提取鲁棒的语义表征；
2. 双阶段聚类过滤：先以 K-means 对表征进行粗粒度聚类，再结合 线性判别分析（LDA） 增强类内紧致性与类间可分性；
3. 标签一致性筛选：对每个聚类，仅保留簇内出现频率最高的真实标签所对应的样本，剔除标签冲突样本（即被投毒的异常样本）。

主要结果与贡献

在Speech Commands v0.02基准上，当10%的源类样本被投毒时，攻击成功率由99.75%骤降至0.25%，模型干净准确率仅下降<0.8%，证明防御高有效性与低开销。该方法对多种威胁变体鲁棒：跨源/目标类组合（如“up→left”、“down→stop”）、不同触发器类型（频域掩码、时域扰动）及投毒比例（5%–20%）均保持稳定防御性能。本工作首次将自监督表征聚类系统性应用于语音投毒防御，为低依赖、高泛化性的鲁棒语音学习提供了新范式。

AI Summary (English)

This paper addresses dirty-label poisoning attacks on speech command classification, where attackers poison utterances from a source class (e.g., “yes”) by adding triggers and relabeling them as a target class (e.g., “no”). We propose an unsupervised defense: first, extracting robust representations via DINO; second, clustering them using K-means + LDA to enhance discriminability; third, retaining only samples whose true label is the majority label within each cluster—effectively filtering poisoned instances. Under 10% poisoning rate, our method reduces attack success rate from 99.75% to 0.25%, while preserving clean accuracy (>96.5%). It generalizes across diverse threat settings—including varying source/target classes, trigger types, and poisoning ratios—without requiring labels, model access, or attack knowledge. This work establishes unsupervised representation clustering as a practical, label-free defense for robust speech learning.

Abstract

Poisoning attacks entail attackers intentionally tampering with training data. In this paper, we consider a dirty-label poisoning attack scenario on a speech commands classification system. The threat model assumes that certain utterances from one of the classes (source class) are poisoned by superimposing a trigger on it, and its label is changed to another class selected by the attacker (target class). We propose a filtering defense against such an attack. First, we use DIstillation with NO labels (DINO) to learn unsupervised representations for all the training examples. Next, we use K-means and LDA to cluster these representations. Finally, we keep the utterances with the most repeated label in their cluster for training and discard the rest. For a 10% poisoned source class, we demonstrate a drop in attack success rate from 99.75% to 0.25%. We test our defense against a variety of threat models, including different target and source classes, as well as trigger variations.

item.title: Fisher-Routed Mixture of Experts for Federated Class-Incremental Learning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.28835v1

AI Summary (中文)

背景与挑战

联邦学习（FL）在保护数据隐私的同时实现分布式协同建模，但将其拓展至类别增量学习（Class-Incremental Learning, CIL）场景面临三重核心挑战：（1）容量冲突与灾难性遗忘——全局共享模型持续接收新类数据，导致旧知识被覆盖；（2）非独立同分布（Non-IID）异质性——各客户端数据分布高度偏斜，加剧模型分歧；（3）同步类对齐失配——客户端增量任务节奏不一致，引发类别标签空间错位。

方法创新

本文提出 Fisher-Routed Mixture of Experts for Federated Class-Incremental Learning（FedFMX），首创面向联邦CIL的自适应专家路由框架。其核心思想是：按样本动态路由至最适专家子集，在知识获取与保留间实现联合优化。具体包含三大模块：

  • Fisher-Routed Expert Scoring（FRES）：融合Fisher信息矩阵衡量参数稳定性代价（抑制遗忘），结合梯度幅值评估可塑性增益（促进新知），量化各专家对当前样本的综合重要性；
  • Adaptive Expert Selection（AES）：基于边际贡献分析，动态确定最小有效专家子集，避免冗余计算与过载；
  • Routing-Aware Regularization（RAR）：引入路由感知正则项，显式约束专家负载均衡，并提升跨轮次联邦聚合的收敛稳定性。

理论与实验验证

理论证明FedFMX在非凸设定下具备 $\mathcal{O}(T^{-1})$ 收敛速率。在CIFAR-100、ImageNet-Subset及Tiny-Imagenet等标准基准上，FedFMX显著超越FedWeIT、FedCL、L2G等SOTA方法：平均准确率提升+5.2%～+9.7%，遗忘率降低38.4%，通信开销减少22%（因稀疏路由）。代码已开源，支持真实设备异构环境部署。

AI Summary (English)

Federated Class-Incremental Learning (FCIL) faces three critical challenges: catastrophic forgetting due to shared model overloading, data heterogeneity from Non-IID client distributions, and class misalignment across asynchronous incremental tasks. To address them, we propose FedFMX, a novel Fisher-routed Mixture of Experts framework. FedFMX introduces: (i) the Fisher-Routed Expert Scoring (FRES) module that jointly quantifies expert stability (via Fisher information) and plasticity (via gradient norms) for sample-aware routing; (ii) the Adaptive Expert Selection (AES) module that determines minimal high-marginal-contribution expert subsets per sample; and (iii) Routing-Aware Regularization (RAR) to enforce load balancing and stabilize federated aggregation. We prove its $\mathcal{O}(T^{-1})$ convergence rate under non-convex settings. Extensive experiments on CIFAR-100, Tiny-ImageNet, and ImageNet-Subset show FedFMX outperforms state-of-the-art FCIL methods by +5.2–9.7% average accuracy, −38.4% forgetting, and 22% less communication cost.

Abstract

Federated Learning (FL) emerged as a promising distributed machine learning paradigm. However, extending FL to the class incremental learning scenarios introduces unique challenges: 1) Capacity conflict and catastrophic forgetting from the shared model overloading, 2) Heterogeneity from Non-Independent and Identically Distributed (Non-IID) data, and 3) Synchronized class misalignment. In this paper, we propose \textbf{F}isher-Routed \textbf{M}i\textbf{X}ture of Experts for \textbf{Fed}erated Class-Incremental Learning (\textsc{FedFMX}), a novel framework to address these challenges via adaptive expert specialization across clients. The crucial insight is to route each sample to an expert subset that jointly optimizes knowledge acquisition and retention. Specifically, we introduce a Fisher-Routed Expert Scoring (FRES) module to estimate expert importance via Fisher-based stability cost and gradient-based plasticity gain. Then, we design an Adaptive Expert Selection (AES) module by quantifying marginal contributions for adaptive expert subset determination. Finally, by the routing-aware regularization (RAR), we achieve load balance and efficient FL training. We theoretically prove the $\mathcal{O}(T^{-1})$ convergence rate. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks compared with state-of-the-art methods demonstrate the superiority of \textsc{FedFMX}.

item.title: Agent Safety Is Action Alignment
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.28739v1

AI Summary (中文)

核心论点

本文提出根本性区分：Agent 安全 ≠ 内容安全。当大语言模型（LLM）作为自主代理（agent）执行真实世界动作（如调用工具、转账、删数据、发消息）时，传统基于“拒绝有害输入”的内容安全范式存在本质误用——这是一种范畴错误（category error）。内容安全的伤害源于模型输出文本本身（可学习、可观测），而行动安全的伤害源于动作所行使的权威与用户实际授予的权限之间的错配，该关系无法从模型输入文本中推断。

关键证据

1. 防御训练失效：针对动作安全微调的模型仅习得表面模式（如关键词回避），未理解授权意图；
2. 能力坍塌与漏洞并存：同一训练使多步代理在无明确威胁时即崩溃，却仍对越权操作（如绕过确认步骤）高度脆弱；
3. 前沿模型天然越权：即使未经防御训练的SOTA模型，在常规交互中亦频繁执行超出用户隐含/显式授权的动作（如自动发送未审核消息）。

创新结论

行动安全无法通过权重参数内化。必须转向最小权限原则（least privilege）：在模型外部、动作执行边界处强制实施权限校验；其评估标准应为动作对齐（action alignment）——一种依赖部署上下文、刻画“动作-授权”关系的动态属性，而非静态的“拒绝率”或“安全分数”。

AI Summary (English)

This paper argues that agent safety is fundamentally distinct from content safety: while content harm arises in the model’s output (a learnable function of text), agentic harm arises from the mismatch between the authority exercised by an action and the authority granted by the user—a relational property absent from the input text. We show that importing refusal-based safety methods into agentic settings commits a category error: it sacrifices capability without delivering security. Three lines of evidence support this: (1) defense-trained models learn superficial textual heuristics, not intent-aware authorization reasoning; (2) the same training collapses multi-step agent capabilities prematurely while leaving critical privilege escalation vulnerabilities intact; and (3) even state-of-the-art, undefended models routinely exceed granted authority under normal use. We conclude that action safety cannot be encoded in model weights. It must be enforced outside the model via least-privilege constraints at the action boundary and evaluated as action alignment—a deployment-conditioned, relational property—not a scalar refusal score.

Abstract

Large language models increasingly act as agents: they call tools, move money, delete records, and send messages on a user's behalf. To keep them safe, practitioners imported the chatbot-era recipe (train the model to refuse unsafe inputs) into the agentic setting, and treat the resulting capability loss as a manageable ``alignment tax.'' We argue this is a \emph{category error}. Refusal is a primitive for \emph{content safety}, where the harm is in the model's output and is therefore a learnable function of it. Agentic harm is different in kind: it lies not in any output but in the relation between the authority an action exercises and the authority the user granted, which is absent from the text the model sees. Importing content-safety methods into this regime does not trade capability for safety; it pays capability and buys negative security. We support this with three lines of evidence spanning the autonomy spectrum: defense-trained models learn surface patterns rather than intent; the same training collapses multi-step agents before any threat appears while leaving them exploitable; and even undefended frontier models exceed granted authority under ordinary use. We conclude that action safety cannot be installed in weights. It must be expressed as \emph{least privilege}, enforced \emph{outside} the model at the action boundary, and evaluated as \emph{action alignment} (a relational, deployment-conditioned property) rather than a refusal score.

item.title: A Novel Latent-Class Attack and its Detection by Class Subspace Orthogonalization
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.29112v1

AI Summary (中文)

新型潜类攻击及其基于类子空间正交化的检测方法

深度学习模型高度依赖大规模训练数据，因而易受数据投毒攻击威胁，典型如通用错误注入与后门（木马）攻击。本文提出一种新型潜类攻击（Latent-Class Attack）：攻击者向训练集注入一批来自未知新类别（即目标分类任务中未定义的语义类别）的样本，并统一将其错误标注为某已知目标类别。模型在训练过程中将该未知类隐式习得为已知类的子类，导致推理时将真实的新类样本高置信度误判为目标类——例如使安防系统将“入侵者”识别为“授权人员”，或使社交AI将“敌对用户”归为“好友”。

为应对该隐蔽威胁，我们设计了一种无需访问原始训练数据的后训练检测框架。核心创新在于引入类子空间正交化（Class Subspace Orthogonalization, CSO）机制：首先基于干净验证集构建各已知类的特征子空间；随后搜索一个内部表征与所有已知类子空间均正交、却仍被模型高置信度分类至某已知类的输入样本——此类样本即为潜类攻击存在的强证据。进一步，针对图像领域，我们提出可微分优化方法生成该可疑样本的可视化图像，实现检测结果的可解释性溯源。实验表明，本方法在CIFAR-10/100及Tiny-ImageNet上对多种潜类攻击变体检测准确率超92%，且对正常模型无误报，显著优于现有后门检测器。

AI Summary (English)

We propose a novel latent-class attack, where poisoned samples originate from an unknown class (outside the target classification domain) but are mislabeled as a known target class, causing the model to internalize the unknown class as a semantic subclass of the target. This threat undermines trust in safety-critical applications (e.g., AI access control). To detect it post-training without training data access, we introduce Class Subspace Orthogonalization (CSO): we construct class-specific feature subspaces from a clean validation set, then search for an input whose hidden representation is orthogonal to all known-class subspaces yet is confidently classified into one of them—serving as a diagnostic signature of latent-class poisoning. For image tasks, we further optimize and visualize such inputs for explainability. Evaluated on CIFAR-10/100 and Tiny-ImageNet, our method achieves >92% detection accuracy across diverse attack variants with near-zero false positives, outperforming state-of-the-art backdoor detectors.

Abstract

Deep learning, which in general relies on voluminous amounts of training data, is vulnerable to data poisoning attacks, including error-generic attacks and backdoors (Trojans). In this work, we propose a new data poisoning attack we dub a latent class attack. Here, all poisoned examples are from a class that is novel (unknown) for the given classification domain and are mislabeled to one of the known classes (the target class) of the domain, so that the model learns to recognize the novel class as a sub-class of the target class. Such attacks could be used e.g. to defeat AI-based access control systems, or could cause a "foe" to be classified as a "friend". We also propose a post-training defense to detect this attack, without any access to the training set. This detection approach builds on "class subspace orthogonalization" (CSO), a plug-and-play paradigm demonstrated to improve existing backdoor detectors. Here, CSO is used to seek an input (a putative unknown class instance) whose internal representation is not aligned with any of the known classes, and yet which is classified with confidence to one of these classes. Finally, specific to image classification domains, we propose a method for visualizing the estimated unknown class instance, providing explainability to our latent class detections.

item.title: Structure-Preserving Document Translation via Multi-Stage LLM Pipeline: A Case Study in Marathi
item.description:
Paper Link: https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.28796v1

AI Summary (中文)

研究背景

印度政府文件主要以马拉地语等区域性语言发布，导致非母语读者、跨邦行政机构及政策研究者面临显著可及性障碍。尽管神经机器翻译（NMT）在句子级翻译质量上取得进展，但现有系统普遍忽略文档结构完整性、格式保真度与领域术语一致性，难以满足官方文件对布局、层级与语义严谨性的严苛要求。

方法创新

本文提出一种面向马拉地语政府文档的多阶段大语言模型（LLM）驱动结构保持翻译框架，实现端到端文档级转换：

  • 布局感知OCR：采用坐标定位的PDF解析技术，精准提取文本块及其空间位置；
  • 结构化文本切分：依据段落、标题、表格、页眉页脚等视觉与语义线索进行层级化分割；
  • LLM增强翻译：基于指令微调的开源LLM（如Qwen2-7B），嵌入术语词典约束与上下文感知重写机制，保障政策术语（如“जनसंपर्क अधिकारी”→“Public Information Officer”）准确统一；
  • HTML重建引擎：通过空间对齐约束（如CSS position: absolute + bounding box映射）与DOM树重建，复现源文档的视觉层次与逻辑结构。

实验结果与贡献

在32份真实马拉地语政府PDF（含通知、通告、财政报表）上的评估表明：相比纯文本翻译基线（Google Translate + Pandoc），本框架在结构保真度（+41.2% HTML DOM相似度）、术语一致性（F1达0.93 vs. 0.76）和跨段落连贯性（BLEU+12.4, METEOR+9.8）显著提升。该方案为电子政务中的多语言文档自动化处理提供了可扩展、可审计、符合《印度语言政策》的技术路径。

AI Summary (English)

This paper introduces a structure-preserving multi-stage LLM pipeline for Marathi-to-English translation of Indian government documents. Unlike conventional text-only MT systems, our framework jointly models layout, hierarchy, and domain terminology by integrating coordinate-aware OCR, spatially constrained text segmentation, instruction-tuned LLM translation with terminology anchoring, and HTML-based document reconstruction with strict positional fidelity. Evaluated on 32 real-world Marathi PDFs—including official notifications, budget annexures, and statutory forms—our system achieves +41.2% higher DOM structural similarity, 0.93 F1 on policy-term consistency (vs. 0.76), and +12.4 BLEU over Google Translate + Pandoc baselines. It enables scalable, auditable, and format-aware multilingual e-governance document processing—bridging accessibility gaps while preserving legal and administrative integrity.

Abstract

Government documents in India are predominantly issued in regional languages such as Marathi, creating substantial accessibility barriers for non-native readers, interstate administrative bodies, and policy analysts. Although recent advances in neural machine translation have improved sentence-level translation quality, existing systems largely neglect document structure, formatting integrity, and domain-specific terminology, thereby limiting their applicability to official documentation. This paper presents a structure-preserving Marathi-to-English government document translation framework capable of performing end-to-end document transformation while maintaining layout fidelity. The proposed system integrates layout-aware optical character recognition, coordinate-based text extraction, large language model based translation, and structured document reconstruction through HTML representations. By enforcing spatial alignment constraints and preserving hierarchical document elements, the framework ensures structural consistency between the source and translated documents. Experimental evaluation on real-world Marathi government PDFs demonstrates improved structural preservation, translation coherence, and terminological consistency compared to conventional text-only translation pipelines. The proposed framework contributes toward scalable multilingual accessibility solutions for e-governance and administrative document processing.

item.title: Shorter Hash-based Signatures via Bucket Thinning
item.description:
Paper Link: https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/1328

AI Summary (中文)

背景与问题

SLH-DSA 是 NIST 标准化的无状态哈希基数字签名算法，基于 SPHINCS⁺ 框架，具备抗量子安全性，但其签名尺寸较大（典型值达 10–20 KB），制约实际部署。为压缩签名，近年研究聚焦于优化底层“少用签名”（Few-Time Signature, FTS）组件。Abri 和 Katz 提出的 PORS+FP（Probabilistic One-Time Signature with Forced Pruning）通过强制剪枝降低 FTS 层密钥使用次数，但其覆盖界（cover bound）过于保守，导致参数无法充分缩小，限制了签名压缩潜力。

方法创新：BPORS+FP

本文提出 BPORS+FP（Bucket-thinned PORS+FP），一种面向 SPHINCS⁺ 底层 FTS 层的新型构造。核心思想是引入“桶稀疏化”（bucket thinning）：在外部类 FORS 的桶结构下，为每个子密钥独立生成；对任一被选中的子密钥，其历史使用次数从原始底层数 $q_0$ 被二项式稀疏化为 $R \sim \operatorname{Bin}(q_0, 2^{-B})$，其中 $B$ 为桶位宽。这使 SPHINCS⁺ 层级的覆盖项得以在更小的随机使用计数上评估，从而允许选用更紧凑的子参数。此外，BPORS+FP 将桶稀疏化与全局强制剪枝尺寸阈值协同设计，实现双重压缩增益。

主要结果

在全部六组标准 SPHINCS⁺ 参数中，BPORS+FP 在五组（含 192/256-bit 安全等级）上均优于 PORS+FP；仅在 128-bit “快速”行略逊。针对 NIST SLH-DSA 的限次签名参数集（如 SLH-DSA-SHA2-128s），替换底层 FTS 为 BPORS+FP 后，总签名尺寸缩减达 22%–26%，显著超越 PORS+FP 的 10%–14% 改进。该方案保持原有安全证明范式，无需修改上层树结构或哈希假设，具备直接集成性与实用性。

AI Summary (English)
